593: // openContextMenu builds a context menu for the currently focused/selected item
594: // (plan or instance) and positions it next to the selected item.
595: func (m *home) openContextMenu() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
596: 	if m.focusSlot == slotNav {
597: 		// Nav panel focused — instance rows get the instance menu,
598: 		// plan headers get the plan menu, everything else is a no-op.
599: 		if inst := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance(); inst != nil {
600: 			// fall through to instance context menu below
601: 		} else if planFile := m.nav.GetSelectedPlanFile(); planFile != "" {
602: 			return m.openPlanContextMenu()
603: 		} else {
604: 			return m, nil
605: 		}
606: 	}
607: 
608: 	// Build instance context menu (reached from nav or other slots)
609: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
610: 	if selected == nil {
611: 		return m, nil
612: 	}
613: 	items := []overlay.ContextMenuItem{
614: 		{Label: "open", Action: "open_instance"},
615: 		{Label: "kill", Action: "kill_instance"},
616: 		{Label: "restart", Action: "restart_instance"},
617: 	}
618: 	if selected.Status == session.Paused {
619: 		items = append(items, overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "resume", Action: "resume_instance"})
620: 	} else {
621: 		items = append(items, overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "pause", Action: "pause_instance"})
622: 	}
623: 	if selected.Started() && selected.Status != session.Paused {
624: 		items = append(items, overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "focus agent", Action: "send_prompt_instance"})
625: 	}
626: 	items = append(items, overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "rename", Action: "rename_instance"})
627: 	items = append(items, overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "push branch", Action: "push_instance"})
628: 	items = append(items, overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "create pr", Action: "create_pr_instance"})
629: 	// Wave task: offer manual completion
630: 	if selected.TaskNumber > 0 {
631: 		if orch, ok := m.waveOrchestrators[selected.PlanFile]; ok && orch.IsTaskRunning(selected.TaskNumber) {
632: 			items = append(items, overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "mark complete", Action: "mark_task_complete"})
633: 		}
634: 	}
635: 	// Position at the left edge of the instance list (middle column)
636: 	x := m.navWidth
637: 	y := 1 + 4 + m.nav.GetSelectedIdx()*4 // PaddingTop(1) + header rows + item offset
638: 	m.contextMenu = overlay.NewContextMenu(x, y, items)
639: 	m.state = stateContextMenu
640: 	return m, nil
641: }
642: 
643: func (m *home) openPlanContextMenu() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
644: 	planFile := m.nav.GetSelectedPlanFile()
645: 	if planFile == "" {
646: 		return m, nil
647: 	}
648: 
649: 	var items []overlay.ContextMenuItem
650: 	if m.planState != nil {
651: 		if entry, ok := m.planState.Plans[planFile]; ok {
652: 			// Offer every forward lifecycle stage from the current state so the
653: 			// user can manually advance through plan → implement → review → done.
654: 			switch entry.Status {
655: 			case planstate.StatusReady, planstate.StatusPlanning:
656: 				items = append(items,
657: 					overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "start plan", Action: "start_plan"},
658: 					overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "start implement", Action: "start_implement"},
659: 					overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "start solo agent", Action: "start_solo"},
660: 					overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "start review", Action: "start_review"},
661: 				)
662: 			case planstate.StatusImplementing:
663: 				items = append(items,
664: 					overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "start implement", Action: "start_implement"},
665: 					overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "start solo agent", Action: "start_solo"},
666: 					overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "start review", Action: "start_review"},
667: 				)
668: 			case planstate.StatusReviewing:
669: 				items = append(items,
670: 					overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "start review", Action: "start_review"},
671: 					overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "mark finished", Action: "mark_plan_done"},
672: 				)
673: 			case planstate.StatusDone:
674: 				items = append(items,
675: 					overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "request review", Action: "request_review"},
676: 					overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "resume implement", Action: "resume_implement"},
677: 				)
678: 			}
679: 		}
680: 	}
681: 	// History plans get an "inspect plan" option to move them to the dead section.
682: 	if m.nav.IsSelectedHistoryPlan() {
683: 		items = append(items,
684: 			overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "inspect plan", Action: "inspect_plan"},
685: 		)
686: 	}
687: 	autoAdvanceLabel := "auto-advance waves: off"
688: 	if m.appConfig != nil && m.appConfig.AutoAdvanceWaves {
689: 		autoAdvanceLabel = "auto-advance waves: on"
690: 	}
691: 	items = append(items,
692: 		overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "chat about this", Action: "chat_about_plan"},
693: 		overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "view plan", Action: "view_plan"},
694: 		overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "rename plan", Action: "rename_plan"},
695: 		overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "set topic", Action: "change_topic"},
696: 		overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: autoAdvanceLabel, Action: "toggle_auto_advance"},
697: 		overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "set status", Action: "set_status"},
698: 		overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "merge to main", Action: "merge_plan"},
699: 		overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "mark done", Action: "mark_plan_done"},
700: 		overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "start over", Action: "start_over_plan"},
701: 		overlay.ContextMenuItem{Label: "cancel plan", Action: "cancel_plan"},
702: 	)
703: 
704: 	x := m.navWidth
705: 	y := 1 + 4 + m.nav.GetSelectedIdx()
706: 	m.contextMenu = overlay.NewContextMenu(x, y, items)
707: 	m.state = stateContextMenu
708: 	return m, nil

710: 
711: // pushSelectedInstance pushes the selected instance's branch changes.
712: func (m *home) pushSelectedInstance() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
713: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
714: 	if selected == nil {
715: 		return m, nil
716: 	}
717: 	capturedTitle := selected.Title
718: 	capturedBranch := selected.Branch
719: 	pushAction := func() tea.Msg {
720: 		worktree, err := selected.GetGitWorktree()
721: 		if err != nil {
722: 			return err
723: 		}
724: 		commitMsg := "update from kas"
725: 		if err := worktree.PushChanges(commitMsg, true); err != nil {
726: 			return err
727: 		}
728: 		m.audit(auditlog.EventGitPush, fmt.Sprintf("pushed branch %s", capturedBranch),
729: 			auditlog.WithInstance(capturedTitle),
730: 		)
731: 		return nil
732: 	}
733: 	message := "push changes from '" + selected.Title + "'?"
734: 	return m, m.confirmAction(message, pushAction)
735: }
736: 
737: // triggerPlanStage handles a user action on a plan lifecycle stage row.
738: // It checks if the stage is locked, applies the concurrency gate for the
739: // implement stage, and then executes the stage transition.
740: func (m *home) triggerPlanStage(planFile, stage string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
741: 	if m.planState == nil {
742: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("no plan state loaded"))
743: 	}
744: 	entry, ok := m.planState.Plans[planFile]
745: 	if !ok {
746: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("missing plan state for %s", planFile))
747: 	}
748: 
749: 	// Backfill branch name for plans created before the branch field existed.
750: 	if entry.Branch == "" {
751: 		entry.Branch = gitpkg.PlanBranchFromFile(planFile)
752: 		if err := m.planState.SetBranch(planFile, entry.Branch); err != nil {
753: 			return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("failed to assign branch for plan: %w", err))
754: 		}
755: 	}
756: 
757: 	// Check if stage is locked
758: 	if isLocked(entry.Status, stage) {
759: 		prev := map[string]string{
760: 			"implement": "plan",
761: 			"review":    "implement",
762: 			"finished":  "review",
763: 		}[stage]
764: 		m.toastManager.Error(fmt.Sprintf("complete '%s' first", prev))
765: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
766: 	}
767: 
768: 	// Concurrency gate for coder stages
769: 	if (stage == "implement" || stage == "solo") && entry.Topic != "" {
770: 		if hasConflict, conflictPlan := m.planState.HasRunningCoderInTopic(entry.Topic, planFile); hasConflict {
771: 			conflictName := planstate.DisplayName(conflictPlan)
772: 			message := fmt.Sprintf("⚠ %s is already running in topic \"%s\"\n\nrunning both plans may cause issues.\ncontinue anyway?", conflictName, entry.Topic)
773: 			proceedAction := func() tea.Msg {
774: 				return planStageConfirmedMsg{planFile: planFile, stage: stage}
775: 			}
776: 			return m, m.confirmAction(message, proceedAction)
777: 		}
778: 	}
779: 
780: 	return m.executePlanStage(planFile, stage)
781: }
782: 
783: // executePlanStage runs the actual stage logic (agent spawn, wave orchestration)
784: // after all gates (lock check, concurrency) have passed. Called directly from
785: // triggerPlanStage on the normal path, and via planStageConfirmedMsg when the
786: // user confirms past the topic-concurrency gate.
787: func (m *home) executePlanStage(planFile, stage string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
788: 	if m.planState == nil {
789: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("no plan state loaded"))
790: 	}
791: 	entry, ok := m.planState.Plans[planFile]
792: 	if !ok {
793: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("missing plan state for %s", planFile))
794: 	}
795: 
796: 	// Backfill branch name for plans created before the branch field existed.
797: 	if entry.Branch == "" {
798: 		entry.Branch = gitpkg.PlanBranchFromFile(planFile)
799: 		if err := m.planState.SetBranch(planFile, entry.Branch); err != nil {
800: 			return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("failed to assign branch for plan: %w", err))
801: 		}
802: 	}
803: 
804: 	switch stage {
805: 	case "plan":
806: 		if err := m.fsm.Transition(planFile, planfsm.PlanStart); err != nil {
807: 			return m, m.handleError(err)
808: 		}
809: 		m.audit(auditlog.EventPlanTransition, string(entry.Status)+" → planning",
810: 			auditlog.WithPlan(planFile))
811: 		m.loadPlanState()
812: 		m.updateSidebarPlans()
813: 		return m.spawnPlanAgent(planFile, "plan", buildPlanPrompt(planstate.DisplayName(planFile), entry.Description))
814: 	case "solo":
815: 		if err := m.fsmSetImplementing(planFile); err != nil {
816: 			return m, m.handleError(err)
817: 		}
818: 		m.audit(auditlog.EventPlanTransition, string(entry.Status)+" → implementing (solo)",
819: 			auditlog.WithPlan(planFile))
820: 		m.loadPlanState()
821: 		m.updateSidebarPlans()
822: 		// Check if plan .md file exists on disk to decide prompt content.
823: 		planName := planstate.DisplayName(planFile)
824: 		planPath := filepath.Join(m.activeRepoPath, "docs", "plans", planFile)
825: 		refFile := ""
826: 		if _, err := os.Stat(planPath); err == nil {
827: 			refFile = planFile
828: 		}
829: 		prompt := buildSoloPrompt(planName, entry.Description, refFile)
830: 		return m.spawnPlanAgent(planFile, "solo", prompt)
831: 	case "implement":
832: 		// Read and parse plan — this also validates wave headers.
833: 		plansDir := filepath.Join(m.activeRepoPath, "docs", "plans")
834: 		content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(plansDir, planFile))
835: 		if err != nil {
836: 			return m, m.handleError(err)
837: 		}
838: 		plan, err := planparser.Parse(string(content))
839: 		if err != nil {
840: 			// No wave headers — revert to planning and respawn the planner with a
841: 			// wave-annotation prompt so the agent adds the required ## Wave sections.
842: 			if setErr := m.fsmRevertToPlanning(planFile); setErr != nil {
843: 				return m, m.handleError(setErr)
844: 			}
845: 			m.loadPlanState()
846: 			m.updateSidebarPlans()
847: 			m.toastManager.Info("plan needs ## Wave headers — respawning planner to annotate.")
848: 			_, spawnCmd := m.spawnPlanAgent(planFile, "plan", buildWaveAnnotationPrompt(planFile))
849: 			return m, tea.Batch(m.toastTickCmd(), func() tea.Msg { return planRefreshMsg{} }, spawnCmd)
850: 		}
851: 
852: 		orch := NewWaveOrchestrator(planFile, plan)
853: 		m.waveOrchestrators[planFile] = orch
854: 
855: 		if err := m.fsmSetImplementing(planFile); err != nil {
856: 			return m, m.handleError(err)
857: 		}
858: 		m.audit(auditlog.EventPlanTransition, string(entry.Status)+" → implementing",
859: 			auditlog.WithPlan(planFile))
860: 		m.loadPlanState()
861: 		m.updateSidebarPlans()
862: 		return m.startNextWave(orch, entry)
863: 	case "review":
864: 		if err := m.fsmSetReviewing(planFile); err != nil {
865: 			return m, m.handleError(err)
866: 		}
867: 		m.audit(auditlog.EventPlanTransition, string(entry.Status)+" → reviewing",
868: 			auditlog.WithPlan(planFile))
869: 		m.loadPlanState()
870: 		m.updateSidebarPlans()
871: 		planName := planstate.DisplayName(planFile)
872: 		reviewPrompt := scaffold.LoadReviewPrompt("docs/plans/"+planFile, planName)
873: 		return m.spawnPlanAgent(planFile, "review", reviewPrompt)
874: 	}
875: 
876: 	// Non-agent stages (finished): mark plan done via FSM.
877: 	if err := m.fsm.Transition(planFile, planfsm.ReviewApproved); err != nil {
878: 		return m, m.handleError(err)
879: 	}
880: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventPlanTransition, string(entry.Status)+" → done",
881: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile))
882: 	m.loadPlanState()
883: 	m.updateSidebarPlans()
884: 	return m, nil
885: }
886: 
887: // validatePlanHasWaves reads a plan file and checks it has ## Wave headers.
888: // Returns an error if the plan lacks wave annotations.
889: func validatePlanHasWaves(plansDir, planFile string) error {
890: 	content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(plansDir, planFile))
891: 	if err != nil {
892: 		return fmt.Errorf("read plan: %w", err)
893: 	}
894: 	_, err = planparser.Parse(string(content))
895: 	return err
896: }
897: 
898: // handleTmuxBrowserAction dispatches actions from the tmux session browser overlay.
899: func (m *home) handleTmuxBrowserAction(action overlay.BrowserAction) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
900: 	switch action {
901: 	case overlay.BrowserDismiss:
902: 		m.tmuxBrowser = nil
903: 		m.state = stateDefault
904: 		return m, nil
905: 
906: 	case overlay.BrowserKill:
907: 		if m.tmuxBrowser == nil {
908: 			return m, nil
909: 		}
910: 		item := m.tmuxBrowser.SelectedItem()
911: 		if item.Name == "" {
912: 			return m, nil
913: 		}
914: 		name := item.Name
915: 		m.tmuxBrowser.RemoveSelected()
916: 		if m.tmuxBrowser.IsEmpty() {
917: 			m.tmuxBrowser = nil
918: 			m.state = stateDefault
919: 		}
920: 		return m, func() tea.Msg {
921: 			killCmd := exec.Command("tmux", "kill-session", "-t", name)
922: 			err := killCmd.Run()
923: 			return tmuxKillResultMsg{name: name, err: err}
924: 		}
925: 
926: 	case overlay.BrowserAdopt:
927: 		if m.tmuxBrowser == nil {
928: 			return m, nil
929: 		}
930: 		item := m.tmuxBrowser.SelectedItem()
931: 		if item.Name == "" {
932: 			return m, nil
933: 		}
934: 		m.tmuxBrowser = nil
935: 		m.state = stateDefault
936: 		return m.adoptOrphanSession(overlay.TmuxBrowserItem{
937: 			Name:  item.Name,
938: 			Title: item.Title,
939: 		})
940: 
941: 	case overlay.BrowserAttach:
942: 		if m.tmuxBrowser == nil {
943: 			return m, nil
944: 		}
945: 		item := m.tmuxBrowser.SelectedItem()
946: 		if item.Name == "" {
947: 			return m, nil
948: 		}
949: 		m.tmuxBrowser = nil
950: 		m.state = stateDefault
951: 		name := item.Name
952: 		return m, tea.ExecProcess(exec.Command("tmux", "attach-session", "-t", name), func(err error) tea.Msg {
953: 			return tmuxAttachReturnMsg{}
954: 		})
955: 
956: 	default:
957: 		return m, nil
958: 	}
959: }
960: 
961: // isLocked returns true if the given stage cannot be triggered given the current plan status.
962: // The context menu already gates which forward stages are offered, so this only
963: // guards against truly nonsensical transitions (e.g. marking "finished" when already done).
964: func isLocked(status planstate.Status, stage string) bool {
965: 	switch stage {
966: 	case "plan", "implement", "solo", "review":
967: 		// Forward progression is always allowed — the FSM helpers
968: 		// (fsmSetImplementing, fsmSetReviewing) walk through intermediate states.
969: 		return false
970: 	case "finished":
971: 		return status != planstate.StatusReviewing
972: 	default:
973: 		return true
974: 	}
975: }

32: func mergeTopicStatus(status ui.TopicStatus, inst *session.Instance, started bool) ui.TopicStatus {
33: 	if started && !inst.Paused() && !inst.PromptDetected {
34: 		status.HasRunning = true
35: 	}
36: 	if inst.Notified {
37: 		status.HasNotification = true
38: 	}
39: 	return status
40: }
41: 
42: func mergePlanStatus(status ui.TopicStatus, inst *session.Instance, started bool) ui.TopicStatus {
43: 	if inst.PlanFile == "" {
44: 		return status
45: 	}
46: 	if started && !inst.Paused() {
47: 		if inst.IsReviewer {
48: 			status.HasNotification = true
49: 		} else {
50: 			status.HasRunning = true
51: 		}
52: 	}
53: 	if inst.Notified && inst.IsReviewer {
54: 		status.HasNotification = true
55: 	}
56: 	return status
57: }

59: // computeStatusBarData builds the StatusBarData from the current app state.
60: func (m *home) computeStatusBarData() ui.StatusBarData {
61: 	data := ui.StatusBarData{
62: 		FocusMode:        m.state == stateFocusAgent,
63: 		TmuxSessionCount: m.tmuxSessionCount,
64: 	}
65: 
66: 	if m.nav == nil {
67: 		if data.Branch == "" {
68: 			data.Branch = "main"
69: 		}
70: 		return data
71: 	}
72: 
73: 	planFile := m.nav.GetSelectedPlanFile()
74: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
75: 
76: 	switch {
77: 	case planFile != "" && m.planState != nil:
78: 		entry, ok := m.planState.Entry(planFile)
79: 		if ok {
80: 			data.Branch = entry.Branch
81: 			data.PlanName = planstate.DisplayName(planFile)
82: 			data.PlanStatus = string(entry.Status)
83: 
84: 			if orch, orchOK := m.waveOrchestrators[planFile]; orchOK {
85: 				waveNum := orch.CurrentWaveNumber()
86: 				totalWaves := orch.TotalWaves()
87: 				if waveNum > 0 {
88: 					data.WaveLabel = fmt.Sprintf("wave %d/%d", waveNum, totalWaves)
89: 					tasks := orch.CurrentWaveTasks()
90: 					data.TaskGlyphs = make([]ui.TaskGlyph, len(tasks))
91: 					for i, task := range tasks {
92: 						switch orch.taskStates[task.Number] {
93: 						case taskComplete:
94: 							data.TaskGlyphs[i] = ui.TaskGlyphComplete
95: 						case taskFailed:
96: 							data.TaskGlyphs[i] = ui.TaskGlyphFailed
97: 						case taskRunning:
98: 							data.TaskGlyphs[i] = ui.TaskGlyphRunning
99: 						default:
100: 							data.TaskGlyphs[i] = ui.TaskGlyphPending
101: 						}
102: 					}
103: 				}
104: 			}
105: 		}
106: 	case selected != nil && selected.Branch != "":
107: 		data.Branch = selected.Branch
108: 		if selected.PlanFile != "" && m.planState != nil {
109: 			entry, ok := m.planState.Entry(selected.PlanFile)
110: 			if ok {
111: 				data.PlanName = planstate.DisplayName(selected.PlanFile)
112: 				data.PlanStatus = string(entry.Status)
113: 			}
114: 		}
115: 	}
116: 
117: 	if data.Branch == "" {
118: 		data.Branch = "main"
119: 	}
120: 
121: 	return data
122: }

124: // computePlanStatuses builds per-plan instance status flags (running/notification)

126: func (m *home) computePlanStatuses() map[string]ui.TopicStatus {

140: // plan list itself hasn't changed. When updateSidebarPlans is also called,

146: // focusSlot constants for readability.
147: // Order matches tab layout: info (first), agent (second), diff (third).
148: const (
149: 	slotNav   = 0
150: 	slotInfo  = 1
151: 	slotAgent = 2
152: 	slotDiff  = 3
153: 	slotCount = 4
154: )
155: 
156: // setFocusSlot updates which pane has focus and syncs visual state.
157: func (m *home) setFocusSlot(slot int) {
158: 	m.focusSlot = slot
159: 	m.nav.SetFocused(slot == slotNav)
160: 	m.menu.SetFocusSlot(slot)
161: 
162: 	// Center pane is focused when any of the 3 center tabs is active.
163: 	centerFocused := slot >= slotInfo && slot <= slotDiff
164: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetFocused(centerFocused)
165: 
166: 	// When focusing a center tab, switch the visible tab to match and track which tab is focused.
167: 	if centerFocused {
168: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(slot - slotInfo) // slotInfo=1 → InfoTab=0, etc.
169: 	}
170: 	// focusedTab always tracks activeTab so the gradient header is visible
171: 	// regardless of which pane has keyboard focus.
172: }
173: 
174: // nextFocusSlot cycles the visible center tab forward (info → agent → diff → info).
175: // The sidebar always retains keyboard focus (focusSlot stays slotNav); only the
176: // displayed tab changes. This is called by Tab and →.
177: func (m *home) nextFocusSlot() {
178: 	switch m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() {
179: 	case ui.InfoTab:
180: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
181: 	case ui.PreviewTab:
182: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.DiffTab)
183: 	default: // ui.DiffTab — wraps to info
184: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
185: 	}
186: }
187: 
188: // prevFocusSlot cycles the visible center tab backward (info → diff → agent → info).
189: // The sidebar always retains keyboard focus (focusSlot stays slotNav).
190: func (m *home) prevFocusSlot() {
191: 	switch m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() {
192: 	case ui.PreviewTab:
193: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
194: 	case ui.DiffTab:
195: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
196: 	default: // ui.InfoTab — wraps to diff
197: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.DiffTab)
198: 	}
199: }
200: 
201: // enterFocusMode enters focus/insert mode and starts the fast preview ticker.
202: // enterFocusMode reuses the existing previewTerminal if it is already attached to
203: // the selected instance — entering focus just toggles key forwarding to the same
204: // terminal. Only spawns a new terminal if none is attached yet (rare fallback).
205: func (m *home) enterFocusMode() tea.Cmd {
206: 	m.tabbedWindow.ClearDocumentMode()
207: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
208: 	if selected == nil || !selected.Started() || selected.Status == session.Paused {
209: 		return nil
210: 	}
211: 
212: 	// If previewTerminal is already attached to this instance, just enter focus mode.
213: 	if m.previewTerminal != nil && m.previewTerminalInstance == selected.Title {
214: 		m.state = stateFocusAgent
215: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetFocusMode(true)
216: 		m.menu.SetFocusMode(true)
217: 		return nil
218: 	}
219: 
220: 	// No terminal yet (shouldn't normally happen) — spawn one synchronously-ish.
221: 	cols, rows := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
222: 	if cols < 10 {
223: 		cols = 80
224: 	}
225: 	if rows < 5 {
226: 		rows = 24
227: 	}
228: 	term, err := selected.NewEmbeddedTerminalForInstance(cols, rows)
229: 	if err != nil {
230: 		return m.handleError(err)
231: 	}
232: 	m.previewTerminal = term
233: 	m.previewTerminalInstance = selected.Title
234: 	m.state = stateFocusAgent
235: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetFocusMode(true)
236: 	m.menu.SetFocusMode(true)
237: 
238: 	return nil
239: }

241: // exitFocusMode resets focus state. previewTerminal stays alive — it continues
242: // rendering in normal preview mode after the user exits focus/insert mode.
243: func (m *home) exitFocusMode() {
244: 	// previewTerminal stays alive — it continues rendering in normal preview mode.
245: 	m.state = stateDefault
246: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetFocusMode(false)
247: 	m.menu.SetFocusMode(false)

250: // switchToTab changes the visible center tab without stealing focus from the sidebar.
251: // The sidebar (slotNav) always retains keyboard focus.
252: func (m *home) switchToTab(name keys.KeyName) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
253: 	var targetTab int
254: 	switch name {
255: 	case keys.KeyTabAgent:
256: 		targetTab = ui.PreviewTab
257: 	case keys.KeyTabDiff:
258: 		targetTab = ui.DiffTab
259: 	case keys.KeyTabInfo:
260: 		targetTab = ui.InfoTab
261: 	default:
262: 		return m, nil
263: 	}
264: 
265: 	if m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() == targetTab {
266: 		return m, nil
267: 	}
268: 
269: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(targetTab)
270: 	return m, nil
271: }

273: // saveAllInstances saves allInstances (all repos) to storage.
274: // No-ops gracefully when storage is nil (e.g. in unit tests).
275: func (m *home) saveAllInstances() error {

277: 		return nil
278: 	}

282: // removeFromAllInstances removes an instance from the master list by title.
283: func (m *home) removeFromAllInstances(title string) {
284: 	for i, inst := range m.allInstances {
285: 		if inst.Title == title {

292: // instanceChanged updates the preview pane, menu, and diff pane based on the selected instance.
293: // It returns a tea.Cmd when an async operation is needed (terminal spawn).
294: func (m *home) instanceChanged() tea.Cmd {
295: 	// selected may be nil
296: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
297: 
298: 	// Clear notification on the previously-viewed instance when the user
299: 	// navigates away. This prevents the item from jumping out of "attention"
300: 	// while the user is still looking at it.
301: 	if m.seenNotified != nil && m.seenNotified != selected {
302: 		m.seenNotified.Notified = false
303: 		m.seenNotified = nil
304: 		m.updateNavPanelStatus()
305: 	}
306: 	if selected != nil && selected.Notified {
307: 		m.seenNotified = selected
308: 	}
309: 
310: 	// Manage preview terminal lifecycle on selection change.
311: 	var spawnCmd tea.Cmd
312: 	if selected == nil || !selected.Started() || selected.Status == session.Paused || selected.Exited {
313: 		// No valid instance (or dead tmux session) — tear down terminal.
314: 		if m.previewTerminal != nil {
315: 			m.previewTerminal.Close()
316: 		}
317: 		m.previewTerminal = nil
318: 		m.previewTerminalInstance = ""
319: 	} else if selected.Title != m.previewTerminalInstance {
320: 		// Different instance selected — swap terminal.
321: 		if m.previewTerminal != nil {
322: 			m.previewTerminal.Close()
323: 		}
324: 		m.previewTerminal = nil
325: 		m.previewTerminalInstance = ""
326: 
327: 		cols, rows := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
328: 		if cols < 10 {
329: 			cols = 80
330: 		}
331: 		if rows < 5 {
332: 			rows = 24
333: 		}
334: 		capturedTitle := selected.Title
335: 		capturedInstance := selected
336: 		spawnCmd = func() tea.Msg {
337: 			term, err := capturedInstance.NewEmbeddedTerminalForInstance(cols, rows)
338: 			return previewTerminalReadyMsg{term: term, instanceTitle: capturedTitle, err: err}
339: 		}
340: 	}
341: 
342: 	m.tabbedWindow.UpdateDiff(selected)
343: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetInstance(selected)
344: 	m.updateInfoPane()
345: 	// Update menu with current instance
346: 	m.menu.SetInstance(selected)
347: 
348: 	// Auto-switch tab based on selection type: plan header → info tab, instance → agent tab.
349: 	// Only auto-switch when the nav panel is focused to avoid hijacking explicit tab selection.
350: 	if m.focusSlot == slotNav {
351: 		if selected != nil {
352: 			m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
353: 		} else if m.nav.IsSelectedPlanHeader() {
354: 			m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
355: 		}
356: 	}
357: 
358: 	// Collect async commands.
359: 	var cmds []tea.Cmd
360: 	if spawnCmd != nil {
361: 		cmds = append(cmds, spawnCmd)
362: 	}
363: 
364: 	if len(cmds) == 0 {
365: 		return nil
366: 	}
367: 	if len(cmds) == 1 {
368: 		return cmds[0]
369: 	}
370: 	return tea.Batch(cmds...)
371: }
372: 
373: // focusInstanceForOverlay selects the given instance in the nav panel and
374: // switches the preview to show its output behind an overlay dialog. The user
375: // can see the agent's terminal behind the overlay to understand context before
376: // responding. Returns a tea.Cmd if an async terminal spawn is needed.
377: func (m *home) focusInstanceForOverlay(inst *session.Instance) tea.Cmd {
378: 	if inst == nil {
379: 		return nil
380: 	}
381: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(inst)
382: 	return m.instanceChanged()
383: }

385: // focusPlanInstanceForOverlay selects the best instance belonging to the given
386: // plan file, preferring running instances. This is used before showing overlay
387: // dialogs about plan lifecycle events (wave completion, etc.) so the user can
388: // see the agent output behind the overlay.
389: func (m *home) focusPlanInstanceForOverlay(planFile string) tea.Cmd {
390: 	var best *session.Instance
391: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
392: 		if inst.PlanFile != planFile {
393: 			continue
394: 		}
395: 		if best == nil {
396: 			best = inst
397: 		}
398: 		// Prefer running instances over ready/paused ones.
399: 		if inst.Status == session.Running && best.Status != session.Running {
400: 			best = inst
401: 		}
402: 	}
403: 	return m.focusInstanceForOverlay(best)
404: }

406: func statusString(s session.Status) string {
407: 	switch s {
408: 	case session.Running:
409: 		return "running"
410: 	case session.Ready:
411: 		return "ready"
412: 	case session.Loading:
413: 		return "loading"
414: 	case session.Paused:
415: 		return "paused"
416: 	default:
417: 		return "unknown"
418: 	}
419: }

421: // updateInfoPaneForPlanHeader populates the info tab when a plan header is selected
422: // (no instance). Shows plan metadata and instance summary counts.
423: func (m *home) updateInfoPaneForPlanHeader() {
424: 	planFile := m.nav.GetSelectedPlanFile()
425: 	if planFile == "" || m.planState == nil {
426: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(ui.InfoData{IsPlanHeaderSelected: true})
427: 		return
428: 	}
429: 	entry, ok := m.planState.Entry(planFile)
430: 	if !ok {
431: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(ui.InfoData{IsPlanHeaderSelected: true})
432: 		return
433: 	}
434: 	data := ui.InfoData{
435: 		IsPlanHeaderSelected: true,
436: 		PlanName:             planstate.DisplayName(planFile),
437: 		PlanStatus:           string(entry.Status),
438: 		PlanTopic:            entry.Topic,
439: 		PlanBranch:           entry.Branch,
440: 	}
441: 	if !entry.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
442: 		data.PlanCreated = entry.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02")
443: 	}
444: 	// Count instances belonging to this plan.
445: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
446: 		if inst.PlanFile != planFile {
447: 			continue
448: 		}
449: 		data.PlanInstanceCount++
450: 		switch {
451: 		case inst.Status == session.Running || inst.Status == session.Loading:
452: 			data.PlanRunningCount++
453: 		case inst.Status == session.Paused:
454: 			data.PlanPausedCount++
455: 		default:
456: 			data.PlanReadyCount++
457: 		}
458: 	}
459: 	// Include wave progress if an orchestrator exists for this plan.
460: 	if orch, ok := m.waveOrchestrators[planFile]; ok {
461: 		data.TotalWaves = orch.TotalWaves()
462: 		data.TotalTasks = orch.TotalTasks()
463: 		tasks := orch.CurrentWaveTasks()
464: 		data.WaveTasks = make([]ui.WaveTaskInfo, len(tasks))
465: 		for i, task := range tasks {
466: 			state := "pending"
467: 			if orch.IsTaskComplete(task.Number) {
468: 				state = "complete"
469: 			} else if orch.IsTaskFailed(task.Number) {
470: 				state = "failed"
471: 			} else if orch.IsTaskRunning(task.Number) {
472: 				state = "running"
473: 			}
474: 			data.WaveTasks[i] = ui.WaveTaskInfo{Number: task.Number, State: state}
475: 		}
476: 	}
477: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(data)
478: }

480: // updateInfoPane refreshes the info tab data from the selected instance or plan header.
481: func (m *home) updateInfoPane() {
482: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
483: 	if selected == nil {
484: 		// No instance selected — check if a plan header is selected.
485: 		if m.nav.IsSelectedPlanHeader() {
486: 			m.updateInfoPaneForPlanHeader()
487: 			return
488: 		}
489: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(ui.InfoData{HasInstance: false})
490: 		return
491: 	}
492: 
493: 	data := ui.InfoData{
494: 		HasInstance: true,
495: 		Title:       selected.Title,
496: 		Program:     selected.Program,
497: 		Branch:      selected.Branch,
498: 		Path:        selected.Path,
499: 		Status:      statusString(selected.Status),
500: 		AgentType:   selected.AgentType,
501: 		TaskNumber:  selected.TaskNumber,
502: 		WaveNumber:  selected.WaveNumber,
503: 	}
504: 
505: 	if !selected.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
506: 		data.Created = selected.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04")
507: 	}
508: 
509: 	if selected.PlanFile != "" {
510: 		if m.planState != nil {
511: 			entry, ok := m.planState.Entry(selected.PlanFile)
512: 			if ok {
513: 				data.HasPlan = true
514: 				data.PlanName = planstate.DisplayName(selected.PlanFile)
515: 				data.PlanDescription = entry.Description
516: 				data.PlanStatus = string(entry.Status)
517: 				data.PlanTopic = entry.Topic
518: 				data.PlanBranch = entry.Branch
519: 				if !entry.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
520: 					data.PlanCreated = entry.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02")
521: 				}
522: 			}
523: 		}
524: 
525: 		if orch, ok := m.waveOrchestrators[selected.PlanFile]; ok {
526: 			data.TotalWaves = orch.TotalWaves()
527: 			data.TotalTasks = orch.TotalTasks()
528: 			tasks := orch.CurrentWaveTasks()
529: 			data.WaveTasks = make([]ui.WaveTaskInfo, len(tasks))
530: 			for i, task := range tasks {
531: 				state := "pending"
532: 				if orch.IsTaskComplete(task.Number) {
533: 					state = "complete"
534: 				} else if orch.IsTaskFailed(task.Number) {
535: 					state = "failed"
536: 				} else if orch.IsTaskRunning(task.Number) {
537: 					state = "running"
538: 				}
539: 				data.WaveTasks[i] = ui.WaveTaskInfo{Number: task.Number, State: state}
540: 			}
541: 		}
542: 	}
543: 
544: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(data)
545: }

547: // loadPlanState reads plan state from the store for the active repo.
548: // Called on user-triggered events (plan creation, repo switch, etc.). The periodic
549: // metadata tick loads plan state in its goroutine instead.
550: // Silently no-ops if the store is not configured.
551: func (m *home) loadPlanState() {
552: 	if m.planStateDir == "" || m.planStore == nil {
553: 		return
554: 	}
555: 	ps, err := planstate.Load(m.planStore, m.planStoreProject, m.planStateDir)
556: 	if err != nil {
557: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not load plan state: %v", err)
558: 		if m.toastManager != nil {
559: 			m.toastManager.Error("plan store error: " + err.Error())
560: 		}
561: 		return
562: 	}
563: 	m.planState = ps
564: }

566: // updateSidebarPlans pushes the current plans into the sidebar using the three-level tree API.
567: func (m *home) updateSidebarPlans() {
568: 	if m.planState == nil {
569: 		m.nav.SetTopicsAndPlans(nil, nil, nil)
570: 		return
571: 	}
572: 
573: 	// Build topic displays
574: 	topicInfos := m.planState.Topics()
575: 	topics := make([]ui.TopicDisplay, 0, len(topicInfos))
576: 	for _, t := range topicInfos {
577: 		plans := m.planState.PlansByTopic(t.Name)
578: 		planDisplays := make([]ui.PlanDisplay, 0, len(plans))
579: 		for _, p := range plans {
580: 			if p.Status == planstate.StatusDone || p.Status == planstate.StatusCancelled {
581: 				continue // finished/cancelled plans handled separately
582: 			}
583: 			planDisplays = append(planDisplays, ui.PlanDisplay{
584: 				Filename:    p.Filename,
585: 				Status:      string(p.Status),
586: 				Description: p.Description,
587: 				Branch:      p.Branch,
588: 				Topic:       p.Topic,
589: 			})
590: 		}
591: 		if len(planDisplays) > 0 {
592: 			topics = append(topics, ui.TopicDisplay{Name: t.Name, Plans: planDisplays})
593: 		}
594: 	}
595: 
596: 	// Build ungrouped plans
597: 	ungroupedInfos := m.planState.UngroupedPlans()
598: 	ungrouped := make([]ui.PlanDisplay, 0, len(ungroupedInfos))
599: 	for _, p := range ungroupedInfos {
600: 		ungrouped = append(ungrouped, ui.PlanDisplay{
601: 			Filename:    p.Filename,
602: 			Status:      string(p.Status),
603: 			Description: p.Description,
604: 			Branch:      p.Branch,
605: 		})
606: 	}
607: 
608: 	// Flatten single-plan topics where topic name matches the plan display name.
609: 	// These don't benefit from a topic header — show the plan directly as ungrouped.
610: 	filtered := topics[:0]
611: 	for _, t := range topics {
612: 		if len(t.Plans) == 1 && t.Name == planstate.DisplayName(t.Plans[0].Filename) {
613: 			ungrouped = append(ungrouped, t.Plans[0])
614: 		} else {
615: 			filtered = append(filtered, t)
616: 		}
617: 	}
618: 	topics = filtered
619: 
620: 	// Build history
621: 	finishedInfos := m.planState.Finished()
622: 	history := make([]ui.PlanDisplay, 0, len(finishedInfos))
623: 	for _, p := range finishedInfos {
624: 		history = append(history, ui.PlanDisplay{
625: 			Filename:    p.Filename,
626: 			Status:      string(p.Status),
627: 			Description: p.Description,
628: 			Branch:      p.Branch,
629: 			Topic:       p.Topic,
630: 		})
631: 	}
632: 
633: 	// Set plan statuses before the rebuild so navPlanSortKey uses
634: 	// up-to-date running/notification flags in a single pass.
635: 	m.nav.SetPlanStatuses(m.computePlanStatuses())
636: 
637: 	m.nav.SetTopicsAndPlans(topics, ungrouped, history)
638: 
639: }
640: 
641: // checkPlanCompletion scans running coder instances for plans that have been
642: // marked "done" by the agent and, if found, transitions them to reviewer sessions.
643: // Returns a cmd to start the reviewer (may be nil).
644: func (m *home) checkPlanCompletion() tea.Cmd {
645: 	if m.planState == nil {
646: 		return nil
647: 	}
648: 	// Guard: if a reviewer already exists for a plan, do not spawn another.
649: 	// The async metadata tick can overwrite m.planState with a stale snapshot
650: 	// that still shows StatusDone after transitionToReview already ran and set
651: 	// StatusReviewing. Without this guard, a second reviewer is spawned.
652: 	reviewerPlans := make(map[string]bool)
653: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
654: 		if inst.IsReviewer && inst.PlanFile != "" {
655: 			reviewerPlans[inst.PlanFile] = true
656: 		}
657: 	}
658: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
659: 		if inst.PlanFile == "" || inst.IsReviewer {
660: 			continue
661: 		}
662: 		if inst.ImplementationComplete {
663: 			continue // already went through review cycle — don't re-trigger
664: 		}
665: 		if reviewerPlans[inst.PlanFile] {
666: 			continue // reviewer already spawned; skip regardless of stale plan state
667: 		}
668: 		if !m.planState.IsDone(inst.PlanFile) {
669: 			continue
670: 		}
671: 		return m.transitionToReview(inst)
672: 	}
673: 	return nil
674: }
675: 
676: // transitionToReview marks a plan as "reviewing", pauses the coder session,
677: // spawns a reviewer session with the reviewer profile, and returns the start cmd.
678: func (m *home) transitionToReview(coderInst *session.Instance) tea.Cmd {
679: 	// Guard: transition via FSM before next tick re-reads disk, preventing double-spawn.
680: 	planFile := coderInst.PlanFile
681: 	if err := m.fsm.Transition(planFile, planfsm.ImplementFinished); err != nil {
682: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not set plan %q to reviewing: %v", planFile, err)
683: 		// Mark complete to break the retry loop — checkPlanCompletion fires
684: 		// every tick and would re-attempt this transition indefinitely.
685: 		coderInst.ImplementationComplete = true
686: 		return nil // FSM rejected — plan is not in implementing state, don't spawn reviewer.
687: 	}
688: 
689: 	// Auto-pause the coder instance — its work is done.
690: 	coderInst.ImplementationComplete = true
691: 	if err := coderInst.Pause(); err != nil {
692: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not pause coder instance for %q: %v", planFile, err)
693: 	}
694: 
695: 	return m.spawnReviewer(planFile)
696: }
697: 
698: // spawnReviewer creates and starts a reviewer session for the given plan,
699: // using the plan's shared worktree so it reviews the actual implementation branch.
700: // Does NOT perform any FSM transition — the caller is responsible for that.
701: // Solo agent plans are excluded — the user ends those manually.
702: func (m *home) spawnReviewer(planFile string) tea.Cmd {
703: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
704: 		if inst.PlanFile == planFile && inst.SoloAgent {
705: 			return nil
706: 		}
707: 	}
708: 	planName := planstate.DisplayName(planFile)
709: 	planPath := "docs/plans/" + planFile
710: 	prompt := scaffold.LoadReviewPrompt(planPath, planName)
711: 
712: 	// Kill any previous reviewer for this plan so the new session gets a fresh
713: 	// tmux session instead of reattaching to a stale/errored one.
714: 	m.killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, session.AgentTypeReviewer)
715: 
716: 	// Resolve the plan's branch for the shared worktree.
717: 	branch := m.planBranch(planFile)
718: 	if branch == "" {
719: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not resolve branch for plan %q", planFile)
720: 		return nil
721: 	}
722: 
723: 	reviewerInst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
724: 		Title:     planName + "-review",
725: 		Path:      m.activeRepoPath,
726: 		Program:   m.programForAgent(session.AgentTypeReviewer),
727: 		PlanFile:  planFile,
728: 		AgentType: session.AgentTypeReviewer,
729: 	})
730: 	if err != nil {
731: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not create reviewer instance for %q: %v", planFile, err)
732: 		return nil
733: 	}
734: 	reviewerInst.IsReviewer = true
735: 	reviewerInst.QueuedPrompt = prompt
736: 	reviewerInst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
737: 
738: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(reviewerInst, m.nav.AddInstance(reviewerInst))
739: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(reviewerInst) // sort-order safe, unlike index arithmetic
740: 
741: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned reviewer for %s", planName),
742: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile),
743: 		auditlog.WithInstance(reviewerInst.Title),
744: 		auditlog.WithAgent(session.AgentTypeReviewer),
745: 	)
746: 
747: 	m.toastManager.Success(fmt.Sprintf("implementation complete → review started for %s", planName))
748: 
749: 	shared := gitpkg.NewSharedPlanWorktree(m.activeRepoPath, branch)
750: 	return func() tea.Msg {
751: 		if err := shared.Setup(); err != nil {
752: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: reviewerInst, err: err}
753: 		}
754: 		if err := m.materializePlanFile(planFile, shared.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
755: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: reviewerInst, err: err}
756: 		}
757: 		err := reviewerInst.StartInSharedWorktree(shared, branch)
758: 		return instanceStartedMsg{instance: reviewerInst, err: err}
759: 	}
760: }
761: 
762: func withOpenCodeModelFlag(program, model string) string {
763: 	model = normalizeOpenCodeModelID(model)
764: 	if model == "" {
765: 		return program
766: 	}
767: 
768: 	tokens := strings.Fields(program)
769: 	if len(tokens) == 0 {
770: 		return program
771: 	}
772: 	if filepath.Base(tokens[0]) != "opencode" {
773: 		return program
774: 	}
775: 
776: 	for i, tok := range tokens {
777: 		if tok == "--model" || tok == "-m" {
778: 			if i+1 < len(tokens) {
779: 				return program
780: 			}
781: 			return program
782: 		}
783: 		if strings.HasPrefix(tok, "--model=") {
784: 			return program
785: 		}
786: 	}
787: 
788: 	return program + " --model " + model
789: }
790: 
791: // programForAgent resolves the program command for a given agent type
792: // (e.g. "coder", "planner") using the kasmos config profile. Falls back to
793: // m.program if no profile is configured.
794: //
795: // For typed agents (coder/planner/reviewer), opencode's own --agent flag
796: // handles model selection via its agent config, so we do NOT append --model.
797: // For ad-hoc instances (no agent type), we append --model since there is no
798: // --agent flag to drive model selection.
799: func (m *home) programForAgent(agentType string) string {
800: 	if m.appConfig == nil {
801: 		return m.program
802: 	}
803: 	// Map agent types to config phases or profile names.
804: 	var profile config.AgentProfile
805: 	switch agentType {
806: 	case session.AgentTypeCoder:
807: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("implementing", m.program)
808: 	case session.AgentTypePlanner:
809: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("planning", m.program)
810: 	case session.AgentTypeReviewer:
811: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("quality_review", m.program)
812: 	case session.AgentTypeFixer:
813: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("fixer", m.program)
814: 	default:
815: 		// Ad-hoc — use the "chat" profile if available.
816: 		if p, ok := m.appConfig.Profiles["chat"]; ok && p.Enabled && p.Program != "" {
817: 			profile = p
818: 		} else {
819: 			return m.program
820: 		}
821: 		// Ad-hoc sessions have no --agent flag, so pass --model explicitly.
822: 		prog := profile.BuildCommand()
823: 		return withOpenCodeModelFlag(prog, profile.Model)
824: 	}
825: 	// Typed agents: opencode handles model via --agent <type> + its own config.
826: 	return profile.BuildCommand()
827: }
828: 
829: func normalizeOpenCodeModelID(model string) string {
830: 	model = strings.TrimSpace(model)
831: 	if model == "" || strings.Contains(model, "/") {
832: 		return model
833: 	}
834: 	if strings.HasPrefix(model, "claude-") {
835: 		return "anthropic/" + model
836: 	}
837: 	return model
838: }

840: // killExistingPlanAgent finds and kills any existing instance for the given plan
841: // and agent type, removing it from both the UI list and persistence list.
842: //
843: // IMPORTANT: Instances are removed from both lists BEFORE killing the tmux
844: // session. This prevents the metadata-tick death-detection from seeing a dead
845: // reviewer in the list and auto-firing ReviewApproved (which would prematurely
846: // mark the plan as done).
847: //
848: // For reviewers, also matches legacy instances that only have IsReviewer set.
849: func (m *home) killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, agentType string) {
850: 	// First pass: identify matching instances by title.
851: 	var titles []string
852: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
853: 		if inst.PlanFile != planFile {
854: 			continue
855: 		}
856: 		match := inst.AgentType == agentType
857: 		// Legacy reviewer instances may only have IsReviewer without AgentType.
858: 		if !match && agentType == session.AgentTypeReviewer && inst.IsReviewer {
859: 			match = true
860: 		}
861: 		if match {
862: 			titles = append(titles, inst.Title)
863: 		}
864: 	}
865: 
866: 	// Second pass: remove from both lists, then kill tmux.
867: 	// Removal first ensures the death-detection tick cannot see the dead instance.
868: 	for _, title := range titles {
869: 		inst := m.nav.RemoveByTitle(title)
870: 		m.removeFromAllInstances(title)
871: 		// Invalidate the preview terminal cache so that a replacement instance
872: 		// with the same title (e.g. a new "applying fixes" coder) gets a fresh
873: 		// terminal instead of showing stale output from the killed session.
874: 		if title == m.previewTerminalInstance {
875: 			if m.previewTerminal != nil {
876: 				m.previewTerminal.Close()
877: 			}
878: 			m.previewTerminal = nil
879: 			m.previewTerminalInstance = ""
880: 		}
881: 		if inst != nil {
882: 			if err := inst.Kill(); err != nil {
883: 				log.WarningLog.Printf("could not kill old %s for %q: %v", agentType, planFile, err)
884: 			}
885: 		}
886: 	}
887: }
888: 
889: // spawnCoderWithFeedback creates and starts a coder session for the given plan,
890: // injecting reviewer feedback into the implementation prompt. Uses the plan's
891: // shared worktree so fixes are applied to the actual implementation branch.
892: // Does NOT perform any FSM transition — the caller is responsible for that.
893: func (m *home) spawnCoderWithFeedback(planFile, feedback string) tea.Cmd {
894: 	planName := planstate.DisplayName(planFile)
895: 	prompt := buildImplementPrompt(planFile)
896: 	if feedback != "" {
897: 		prompt += fmt.Sprintf("\n\nReviewer feedback from previous round:\n%s", feedback)
898: 	}
899: 
900: 	// Kill any previous coder for this plan so the new session gets a fresh
901: 	// tmux session instead of reattaching to a stale/errored one.
902: 	m.killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, session.AgentTypeCoder)
903: 
904: 	// Clear the push-prompt dedup flag so this new coder round can trigger
905: 	// the push dialog when it finishes.
906: 	delete(m.coderPushPrompted, planFile)
907: 
908: 	// Resolve the plan's branch for the shared worktree.
909: 	branch := m.planBranch(planFile)
910: 	if branch == "" {
911: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not resolve branch for plan %q", planFile)
912: 		return nil
913: 	}
914: 
915: 	coderInst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
916: 		Title:     planName + "-implement",
917: 		Path:      m.activeRepoPath,
918: 		Program:   m.programForAgent(session.AgentTypeCoder),
919: 		PlanFile:  planFile,
920: 		AgentType: session.AgentTypeCoder,
921: 	})
922: 	if err != nil {
923: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not create coder instance for %q: %v", planFile, err)
924: 		return nil
925: 	}
926: 	coderInst.QueuedPrompt = prompt
927: 	coderInst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
928: 
929: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(coderInst, m.nav.AddInstance(coderInst))
930: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(coderInst)
931: 
932: 	detail := ""
933: 	if feedback != "" {
934: 		if len(feedback) > 200 {
935: 			detail = feedback[:200] + "..."
936: 		} else {
937: 			detail = feedback
938: 		}
939: 	}
940: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned coder with reviewer feedback for %s", planName),
941: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile),
942: 		auditlog.WithInstance(coderInst.Title),
943: 		auditlog.WithAgent(session.AgentTypeCoder),
944: 		auditlog.WithDetail(detail),
945: 	)
946: 
947: 	m.toastManager.Info(fmt.Sprintf("review changes requested → re-implementing %s", planName))
948: 
949: 	shared := gitpkg.NewSharedPlanWorktree(m.activeRepoPath, branch)
950: 	return func() tea.Msg {
951: 		if err := shared.Setup(); err != nil {
952: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: coderInst, err: err}
953: 		}
954: 		if err := m.materializePlanFile(planFile, shared.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
955: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: coderInst, err: err}
956: 		}
957: 		err := coderInst.StartInSharedWorktree(shared, branch)
958: 		return instanceStartedMsg{instance: coderInst, err: err}
959: 	}
960: }

962: func (m *home) materializePlanFile(planFile, repoPath string) error {
963: 	if m.planStore == nil {
964: 		return nil
965: 	}
966: 	content, err := m.planStore.GetContent(m.planStoreProject, planFile)
967: 	if err != nil {
968: 		return fmt.Errorf("get plan content %s: %w", planFile, err)
969: 	}
970: 	plansDir := filepath.Join(repoPath, "docs", "plans")
971: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(plansDir, 0o755); err != nil {
972: 		return fmt.Errorf("create plans dir: %w", err)
973: 	}
974: 	planPath := filepath.Join(plansDir, planFile)
975: 	if err := os.WriteFile(planPath, []byte(content), 0o644); err != nil {
976: 		return fmt.Errorf("write plan file: %w", err)
977: 	}
978: 	return nil
979: }

981: // ingestPlanContent reads the plan markdown file from the given repo path and
982: // stores its content in the DB via the plan store. This bridges the gap between
983: // agents writing plan files to their worktree and the DB being the source of truth.
984: // Called when a PlannerFinished signal is processed.
985: func (m *home) ingestPlanContent(planFile, repoPath string) {
986: 	if m.planStore == nil || m.planState == nil {
987: 		return
988: 	}
989: 	planPath := filepath.Join(repoPath, "docs", "plans", planFile)
990: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(planPath)
991: 	if err != nil {
992: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("ingestPlanContent: cannot read %s: %v", planPath, err)
993: 		return
994: 	}
995: 	if err := m.planState.SetContent(planFile, string(data)); err != nil {
996: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("ingestPlanContent: cannot store content for %s: %v", planFile, err)
997: 	}
998: }

1000: // viewSelectedPlan renders the selected plan's markdown in the preview pane.
1001: // The rendered output is cached; on cache miss the glamour render runs async
1002: // via a tea.Cmd so the UI stays responsive.
1003: func (m *home) viewSelectedPlan() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1004: 	planFile := m.nav.GetSelectedPlanFile()
1005: 	if planFile == "" {
1006: 		return m, nil
1007: 	}
1008: 
1009: 	// Cache hit — reuse previously rendered content (instant).
1010: 	if planFile == m.cachedPlanFile && m.cachedPlanRendered != "" {
1011: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
1012: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetDocumentContent(m.cachedPlanRendered)
1013: 		return m, nil
1014: 	}
1015: 
1016: 	// Cache miss — render async so the UI doesn't freeze.
1017: 	previewWidth, _ := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
1018: 	wordWrap := previewWidth - 4
1019: 	if wordWrap < 40 {
1020: 		wordWrap = 40
1021: 	}
1022: 
1023: 	return m, func() tea.Msg {
1024: 		data, err := m.planStore.GetContent(m.planStoreProject, planFile)
1025: 		if err != nil {
1026: 			return planRenderedMsg{err: fmt.Errorf("could not read plan %s: %w", planFile, err)}
1027: 		}
1028: 
1029: 		renderer, err := glamour.NewTermRenderer(
1030: 			glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"),
1031: 			glamour.WithWordWrap(wordWrap),
1032: 		)
1033: 		if err != nil {
1034: 			return planRenderedMsg{err: fmt.Errorf("could not create markdown renderer: %w", err)}
1035: 		}
1036: 
1037: 		rendered, err := renderer.Render(data)
1038: 		if err != nil {
1039: 			return planRenderedMsg{err: fmt.Errorf("could not render markdown: %w", err)}
1040: 		}
1041: 
1042: 		return planRenderedMsg{planFile: planFile, rendered: rendered}
1043: 	}
1044: }

1046: // createPlanEntry creates a new plan entry in the store.
1047: func (m *home) createPlanEntry(name, description, topic string) error {
1048: 	if m.planState == nil {
1049: 		if m.planStore == nil {
1050: 			return fmt.Errorf("plan store not configured")
1051: 		}
1052: 		ps, err := planstate.Load(m.planStore, m.planStoreProject, m.planStateDir)
1053: 		if err != nil {
1054: 			return err
1055: 		}
1056: 		m.planState = ps
1057: 	}
1058: 
1059: 	slug := slugifyPlanName(name)
1060: 	filename := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s.md", time.Now().UTC().Format("2006-01-02"), slug)
1061: 	branch := "plan/" + slug
1062: 	if err := m.planState.Create(filename, description, branch, topic, time.Now().UTC()); err != nil {
1063: 		if m.toastManager != nil {
1064: 			m.toastManager.Error("plan store error: " + err.Error())
1065: 		}
1066: 		return err
1067: 	}
1068: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventPlanCreated, "created plan", auditlog.WithPlan(filename))
1069: 	m.updateSidebarPlans()
1070: 	return nil
1071: }

1073: // slugifyPlanName converts a plan name to a URL-safe slug.
1074: func slugifyPlanName(name string) string {
1075: 	name = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(name))
1076: 	name = regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-z0-9]+`).ReplaceAllString(name, "-")
1077: 	return strings.Trim(name, "-")
1078: }

1080: // buildPlanFilename derives the plan filename from a human name and creation time.
1081: // "Auth Refactor" → "2026-02-21-auth-refactor.md"
1082: func buildPlanFilename(name string, now time.Time) string {
1083: 	slug := slugifyPlanName(name)
1084: 	if slug == "" {
1085: 		slug = "plan"
1086: 	}
1087: 	return now.UTC().Format("2006-01-02") + "-" + slug + ".md"
1088: }

1090: // renderPlanStub returns the initial markdown content for a new plan file.
1091: func renderPlanStub(name, description, filename string) string {
1092: 	return fmt.Sprintf("# %s\n\n## Context\n\n%s\n\n## Notes\n\n- Created by kas lifecycle flow\n- Plan file: %s\n", name, description, filename)
1093: }

1095: // createPlanRecord registers the plan in the store.
1096: func (m *home) createPlanRecord(planFile, description, branch string, now time.Time) error {
1097: 	if m.planState == nil {
1098: 		if m.planStore == nil {
1099: 			return fmt.Errorf("plan store not configured")
1100: 		}
1101: 		ps, err := planstate.Load(m.planStore, m.planStoreProject, m.planStateDir)
1102: 		if err != nil {
1103: 			return err
1104: 		}
1105: 		m.planState = ps
1106: 	}
1107: 	if err := m.planState.Register(planFile, description, branch, now); err != nil {
1108: 		if m.toastManager != nil {
1109: 			m.toastManager.Error("plan store error: " + err.Error())
1110: 		}
1111: 		return err
1112: 	}
1113: 	return nil
1114: }

1116: // finalizePlanCreation writes the plan stub content to the store, registers it,
1117: // and creates the feature branch. Called at the end of the plan creation wizard.
1118: func (m *home) finalizePlanCreation(name, description string) error {
1119: 	now := time.Now().UTC()
1120: 	planFile := buildPlanFilename(name, now)
1121: 	branch := gitpkg.PlanBranchFromFile(planFile)
1122: 	content := renderPlanStub(name, description, planFile)
1123: 	if err := m.createPlanRecord(planFile, description, branch, now); err != nil {
1124: 		return err
1125: 	}
1126: 	if err := m.planState.SetContent(planFile, content); err != nil {
1127: 		return err
1128: 	}
1129: 	if err := gitpkg.EnsurePlanBranch(m.activeRepoPath, branch); err != nil {
1130: 		return err
1131: 	}
1132: 
1133: 	m.loadPlanState()
1134: 	m.updateSidebarPlans()
1135: 	return nil
1136: }

1138: func (m *home) importClickUpTask(task *clickup.Task) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1139: 	if task == nil {
1140: 		m.toastManager.Error("clickup fetch failed: empty task payload")
1141: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
1142: 	}
1143: 
1144: 	date := time.Now().Format("2006-01-02")
1145: 	filename := clickup.ScaffoldFilename(task.Name, date)
1146: 
1147: 	if m.planState == nil {
1148: 		m.loadPlanState()
1149: 	}
1150: 	if m.planState == nil {
1151: 		m.toastManager.Error("failed to register imported plan: plan state unavailable")
1152: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
1153: 	}
1154: 
1155: 	filename = dedupePlanFilenameInState(m.planState, filename)
1156: 
1157: 	scaffold := clickup.ScaffoldPlan(*task)
1158: 
1159: 	branch := gitpkg.PlanBranchFromFile(filename)
1160: 	if err := m.planState.Register(filename, task.Name, branch, time.Now()); err != nil {
1161: 		m.toastManager.Error("failed to register imported plan: " + err.Error())
1162: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
1163: 	}
1164: 	if err := m.planState.SetContent(filename, scaffold); err != nil {
1165: 		m.toastManager.Error("failed to save imported plan content: " + err.Error())
1166: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
1167: 	}
1168: 	if task.ID != "" {
1169: 		if err := m.planState.SetClickUpTaskID(filename, task.ID); err != nil {
1170: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("importClickUpTask: failed to set clickup task id for %q: %v", filename, err)
1171: 		}
1172: 	}
1173: 
1174: 	if err := m.fsm.Transition(filename, planfsm.PlanStart); err != nil {
1175: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("clickup import transition failed for %q: %v", filename, err)
1176: 	}
1177: 
1178: 	m.loadPlanState()
1179: 	m.updateSidebarPlans()
1180: 
1181: 	prompt := fmt.Sprintf(`Analyze this imported ClickUp task. The task details and subtasks are included as reference in the plan file.
1182: 
1183: Determine if the task is well-specified enough for implementation or needs further analysis. Write a proper implementation plan with ## Wave sections, task breakdowns, architecture notes, and tech stack. Use the ClickUp subtasks as a starting point but reorganize into waves based on dependencies.
1184: 
1185: The plan file is at: docs/plans/%s`, filename)
1186: 
1187: 	m.toastManager.Success("imported! spawning planner...")
1188: 	model, cmd := m.spawnPlanAgent(filename, "plan", prompt)
1189: 	if cmd == nil {
1190: 		return model, m.toastTickCmd()
1191: 	}
1192: 	return model, tea.Batch(cmd, m.toastTickCmd())
1193: }
1194: 
1195: func dedupePlanFilename(plansDir, filename string) string {
1196: 	planPath := filepath.Join(plansDir, filename)
1197: 	if _, err := os.Stat(planPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
1198: 		return filename
1199: 	}
1200: 
1201: 	base := strings.TrimSuffix(filename, ".md")
1202: 	for i := 2; i < 100; i++ {
1203: 		candidate := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%d.md", base, i)
1204: 		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(plansDir, candidate)); os.IsNotExist(err) {
1205: 			return candidate
1206: 		}
1207: 	}
1208: 
1209: 	return filename
1210: }
1211: 
1212: func dedupePlanFilenameInState(ps *planstate.PlanState, filename string) string {
1213: 	if ps == nil {
1214: 		return filename
1215: 	}
1216: 	if _, ok := ps.Entry(filename); !ok {
1217: 		return filename
1218: 	}
1219: 
1220: 	base := strings.TrimSuffix(filename, ".md")
1221: 	for i := 2; i < 100; i++ {
1222: 		candidate := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%d.md", base, i)
1223: 		if _, ok := ps.Entry(candidate); !ok {
1224: 			return candidate
1225: 		}
1226: 	}
1227: 
1228: 	return filename
1229: }
1230: 
1231: // shouldPromptPushAfterCoderExit returns true when a coder session has finished
1232: // and the plan is still in the implementing state. A coder is considered finished
1233: // when its tmux session has exited (tmuxAlive == false) OR when it has returned
1234: // to a prompt after completing its queued work (PromptDetected && !AwaitingWork).
1235: // The latter covers "applying fixes" coders spawned by spawnCoderWithFeedback,
1236: // which finish their work and return to prompt rather than exiting tmux.
1237: func shouldPromptPushAfterCoderExit(entry planstate.PlanEntry, inst *session.Instance, tmuxAlive bool) bool {
1238: 	if inst == nil {
1239: 		return false
1240: 	}
1241: 	if inst.PlanFile == "" {
1242: 		return false
1243: 	}
1244: 	if inst.AgentType != session.AgentTypeCoder {
1245: 		return false
1246: 	}
1247: 	if inst.SoloAgent {
1248: 		return false
1249: 	}
1250: 	if entry.Status != planstate.StatusImplementing {
1251: 		return false
1252: 	}
1253: 	// Tmux exited — original single-coder completion path.
1254: 	if !tmuxAlive {
1255: 		return true
1256: 	}
1257: 	// Agent returned to prompt after finishing queued work — covers the
1258: 	// review-feedback coder ("applying fixes") which stays alive in tmux.
1259: 	if inst.PromptDetected && !inst.AwaitingWork {
1260: 		return true
1261: 	}
1262: 	return false
1263: }
1264: 
1265: // promptPushBranchThenAdvance shows a confirmation overlay asking the user to
1266: // push the implementation branch, then advances the plan to reviewing and
1267: // spawns a reviewer agent via coderCompleteMsg.
1268: func (m *home) promptPushBranchThenAdvance(inst *session.Instance) tea.Cmd {
1269: 	capturedPlanFile := inst.PlanFile
1270: 	capturedTitle := inst.Title
1271: 	// Mark as prompted so the metadata tick doesn't re-trigger the dialog
1272: 	// while the user is deciding or after they dismiss it.
1273: 	if m.coderPushPrompted == nil {
1274: 		m.coderPushPrompted = make(map[string]bool)
1275: 	}
1276: 	m.coderPushPrompted[capturedPlanFile] = true
1277: 	message := fmt.Sprintf("[!] implementation finished for '%s'. push branch now?", planstate.DisplayName(capturedPlanFile))
1278: 	pushAction := func() tea.Msg {
1279: 		worktree, err := inst.GetGitWorktree()
1280: 		if err == nil {
1281: 			_ = worktree.PushChanges(
1282: 				fmt.Sprintf("[kas] push completed implementation for '%s'", capturedTitle),
1283: 				false,
1284: 			)
1285: 		}
1286: 		return coderCompleteMsg{planFile: capturedPlanFile}
1287: 	}
1288: 	return m.confirmAction(message, func() tea.Msg { return pushAction() })
1289: }
1290: 
1291: // planBranch resolves the branch name for a plan, backfilling if needed.
1292: func (m *home) planBranch(planFile string) string {
1293: 	if m.planState == nil {
1294: 		return ""
1295: 	}
1296: 	entry, ok := m.planState.Entry(planFile)
1297: 	if !ok {
1298: 		return ""
1299: 	}
1300: 	if entry.Branch == "" {
1301: 		entry.Branch = gitpkg.PlanBranchFromFile(planFile)
1302: 		_ = m.planState.SetBranch(planFile, entry.Branch)
1303: 	}
1304: 	return entry.Branch
1305: }

1307: // buildPlanPrompt returns the initial prompt for a planner agent session.
1308: // The prompt explicitly requires ## Wave N headers because kasmos uses them
1309: // for wave orchestration — without them, implementation cannot start.
1310: func buildPlanPrompt(planName, description string) string {
1311: 	return fmt.Sprintf(
1312: 		"Plan %s. Goal: %s. "+
1313: 			"Use the `kasmos-planner` skill. "+
1314: 			"The plan MUST include ## Wave N sections (at minimum ## Wave 1) "+
1315: 			"grouping all tasks — kasmos requires Wave headers to orchestrate implementation.",
1316: 		planName, description,
1317: 	)
1318: }
1319: 
1320: // buildWaveAnnotationPrompt returns the prompt used when a planner is respawned
1321: // to add ## Wave headers to an existing plan that is missing them.
1322: // It instructs the planner to annotate the plan, commit the change, and write
1323: // the sentinel signal so kasmos can resume the implementation flow.
1324: func buildWaveAnnotationPrompt(planFile string) string {
1325: 	return fmt.Sprintf(
1326: 		"The plan at docs/plans/%[1]s is missing ## Wave N headers required for kasmos wave orchestration. "+
1327: 			"Please annotate the plan by wrapping all tasks under ## Wave N sections. "+
1328: 			"Every plan needs at least ## Wave 1 — even single-task trivial plans. "+
1329: 			"Keep all existing task content intact; only add the ## Wave headers.\n\n"+
1330: 			"After annotating:\n"+
1331: 			"1. Commit: git add docs/plans/%[1]s && git commit -m \"plan: add wave headers to %[1]s\"\n"+
1332: 			"2. Signal completion: touch .kasmos/signals/planner-finished-%[1]s\n"+
1333: 			"Do not edit plan-state.json directly.",
1334: 		planFile,
1335: 	)
1336: }
1337: 
1338: // buildImplementPrompt returns the prompt for a coder agent session.
1339: // The plan file is materialized to docs/plans/<planFile> in the agent's worktree
1340: // by the TUI before the agent starts. Agents write sentinel signals to
1341: // .kasmos/signals/ in their worktree; the TUI ingests them on completion.
1342: func buildImplementPrompt(planFile string) string {
1343: 	return fmt.Sprintf(
1344: 		"Implement docs/plans/%s using the `kasmos-coder` skill. Execute all tasks sequentially.",
1345: 		planFile,
1346: 	)
1347: }
1348: 
1349: // buildSoloPrompt returns a minimal prompt for a solo agent session.
1350: // If planFile is non-empty, it references the plan file. Otherwise just name + description.
1351: func buildSoloPrompt(planName, description, planFile string) string {
1352: 	if planFile != "" {
1353: 		return fmt.Sprintf("Implement %s. Goal: %s. Plan: docs/plans/%s", planName, description, planFile)
1354: 	}
1355: 	return fmt.Sprintf("Implement %s. Goal: %s.", planName, description)
1356: }
1357: 
1358: // buildModifyPlanPrompt returns the prompt for modifying an existing plan.
1359: func buildModifyPlanPrompt(planFile string) string {
1360: 	return fmt.Sprintf("Modify existing plan at docs/plans/%s. Keep the same filename and update only what changed.", planFile)
1361: }
1362: 
1363: // agentTypeForSubItem maps a sidebar stage name to the corresponding AgentType constant.
1364: func agentTypeForSubItem(action string) (string, bool) {
1365: 	switch action {
1366: 	case "plan":
1367: 		return session.AgentTypePlanner, true
1368: 	case "implement", "solo":
1369: 		return session.AgentTypeCoder, true
1370: 	case "review":
1371: 		return session.AgentTypeReviewer, true
1372: 	default:
1373: 		return "", false
1374: 	}
1375: }
1376: 
1377: // spawnAdHocAgent creates and starts an ad-hoc agent session (no plan, no lifecycle).
1378: // branch and workPath are optional overrides - empty strings use defaults.
1379: func (m *home) spawnAdHocAgent(name, branch, workPath string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1380: 	path := m.activeRepoPath
1381: 	if workPath != "" {
1382: 		path = workPath
1383: 	}
1384: 
1385: 	inst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
1386: 		Title:   name,
1387: 		Path:    path,
1388: 		Program: m.programForAgent(session.AgentTypeFixer),
1389: 	})
1390: 	if err != nil {
1391: 		return m, m.handleError(err)
1392: 	}
1393: 
1394: 	inst.AgentType = session.AgentTypeFixer
1395: 	inst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
1396: 	inst.LoadingTotal = 8
1397: 	inst.LoadingMessage = "preparing session..."
1398: 
1399: 	m.state = stateDefault
1400: 	m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
1401: 
1402: 	var startCmd tea.Cmd
1403: 	switch {
1404: 	case workPath != "" && branch == "":
1405: 		// Path override only - run in-place on main branch (no worktree)
1406: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1407: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: inst.StartOnMainBranch()}
1408: 		}
1409: 
1410: 	case branch != "":
1411: 		// Branch override - create worktree on specified branch
1412: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1413: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: inst.StartOnBranch(branch)}
1414: 		}
1415: 
1416: 	default:
1417: 		// No overrides - run in-place on current branch (no worktree)
1418: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1419: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: inst.StartOnMainBranch()}
1420: 		}
1421: 	}
1422: 
1423: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned fixer agent: %s", name),
1424: 		auditlog.WithInstance(name),
1425: 		auditlog.WithAgent(session.AgentTypeFixer),
1426: 	)
1427: 
1428: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(inst, m.nav.AddInstance(inst))
1429: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(inst)
1430: 	return m, tea.Batch(tea.WindowSize(), startCmd)
1431: }
1432: 
1433: // spawnPlanAgent creates and starts an agent session for the given plan and action.
1434: func (m *home) spawnPlanAgent(planFile, action, prompt string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1435: 	entry, ok := m.planState.Entry(planFile)
1436: 	if !ok {
1437: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", planFile))
1438: 	}
1439: 
1440: 	agentType, ok := agentTypeForSubItem(action)
1441: 	if !ok {
1442: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("unknown plan action: %s", action))
1443: 	}
1444: 
1445: 	// Kill any existing instance of the same type for this plan to prevent
1446: 	// duplicates (e.g. user triggers "start review" when a reviewer already exists).
1447: 	if agentType == session.AgentTypeReviewer || agentType == session.AgentTypePlanner {
1448: 		m.killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, agentType)
1449: 	}
1450: 
1451: 	title := planstate.DisplayName(planFile) + "-" + action
1452: 	if action == "solo" {
1453: 		// Solo sessions run on main branch, so their tmux session names must stay
1454: 		// unique to avoid accidentally reattaching to another solo session.
1455: 		title = planstate.DisplayName(planFile) + "-solo"
1456: 	}
1457: 	inst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
1458: 		Title:     title,
1459: 		Path:      m.activeRepoPath,
1460: 		Program:   m.programForAgent(agentType),
1461: 		PlanFile:  planFile,
1462: 		AgentType: agentType,
1463: 	})
1464: 	if err != nil {
1465: 		return m, m.handleError(err)
1466: 	}
1467: 	// Keep IsReviewer in sync with AgentType so the reviewer-completion check
1468: 	// in the metadata tick handler (which gates on inst.IsReviewer) fires for
1469: 	// sidebar-spawned reviewers as well as auto-spawned ones.
1470: 	if agentType == session.AgentTypeReviewer {
1471: 		inst.IsReviewer = true
1472: 	}
1473: 	if action == "solo" {
1474: 		inst.SoloAgent = true
1475: 	}
1476: 	inst.QueuedPrompt = prompt
1477: 
1478: 	// Set loading state immediately so the UI shows the progress bar
1479: 	// instead of the idle banner while the async start runs.
1480: 	inst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
1481: 	inst.LoadingTotal = 5
1482: 	inst.LoadingMessage = "Preparing session..."
1483: 
1484: 	var startCmd tea.Cmd
1485: 	if action == "plan" || action == "solo" {
1486: 		// Planner and solo agent run on main branch — no worktree created.
1487: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1488: 			if err := m.materializePlanFile(planFile, m.activeRepoPath); err != nil {
1489: 				return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1490: 			}
1491: 			err := inst.StartOnMainBranch()
1492: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1493: 		}
1494: 	} else {
1495: 		// Backfill branch name for plans created before the branch field was introduced.
1496: 		if entry.Branch == "" {
1497: 			entry.Branch = gitpkg.PlanBranchFromFile(planFile)
1498: 			if err := m.planState.SetBranch(planFile, entry.Branch); err != nil {
1499: 				return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("failed to assign branch for plan: %w", err))
1500: 			}
1501: 		}
1502: 
1503: 		// Coder and reviewer share the plan's feature branch worktree
1504: 		shared := gitpkg.NewSharedPlanWorktree(m.activeRepoPath, entry.Branch)
1505: 		if err := shared.Setup(); err != nil {
1506: 			return m, m.handleError(err)
1507: 		}
1508: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1509: 			if err := m.materializePlanFile(planFile, shared.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
1510: 				return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1511: 			}
1512: 			err := inst.StartInSharedWorktree(shared, entry.Branch)
1513: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1514: 		}
1515: 	}
1516: 
1517: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned %s for plan %s", agentType, planstate.DisplayName(planFile)),
1518: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile),
1519: 		auditlog.WithInstance(title),
1520: 		auditlog.WithAgent(agentType),
1521: 	)
1522: 
1523: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(inst, m.nav.AddInstance(inst))
1524: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(inst)
1525: 	return m, tea.Batch(tea.WindowSize(), startCmd)
1526: }
1527: 
1528: // getTopicNames returns existing topic names for the picker.
1529: func (m *home) getTopicNames() []string {
1530: 	if m.planState == nil {
1531: 		return nil
1532: 	}
1533: 	topics := m.planState.Topics()
1534: 	names := make([]string, len(topics))
1535: 	for i, t := range topics {
1536: 		names[i] = t.Name
1537: 	}
1538: 	return names
1539: }
1540: 
1541: // rebuildOrphanedOrchestrators reconstructs in-memory WaveOrchestrators for plans that
1542: // were mid-wave when kasmos was restarted. Without this, the wave completion monitor and
1543: // the "Mark complete" context menu action are both inoperative after a restart.
1544: //
1545: // For each plan that is StatusImplementing and has task instances (TaskNumber > 0) but
1546: // no orchestrator, we:
1547: //  1. Parse the plan file to get the wave/task structure.

<content>1: package planfsm
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 
6: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/planstate"
7: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/planstore"
8: )
9: 
10: // Status represents the lifecycle state of a plan.
11: type Status string
12: 
13: const (
14: 	StatusReady        Status = "ready"
15: 	StatusPlanning     Status = "planning"
16: 	StatusImplementing Status = "implementing"
17: 	StatusReviewing    Status = "reviewing"
18: 	StatusDone         Status = "done"
19: 	StatusCancelled    Status = "cancelled"
20: )
21: 
22: // Event represents a lifecycle transition trigger.
23: type Event string
24: 
25: const (
26: 	PlanStart              Event = "plan_start"
27: 	PlannerFinished        Event = "planner_finished"
28: 	ImplementStart         Event = "implement_start"
29: 	ImplementFinished      Event = "implement_finished"
30: 	ReviewApproved         Event = "review_approved"
31: 	ReviewChangesRequested Event = "review_changes_requested"
32: 	RequestReview          Event = "request_review"
33: 	StartOver              Event = "start_over"
34: 	Reimplement            Event = "reimplement"
35: 	Cancel                 Event = "cancel"
36: 	Reopen                 Event = "reopen"
37: )
38: 
39: // IsUserOnly returns true if this event can only be triggered from the TUI,
40: // never by agent sentinel files.
41: func (e Event) IsUserOnly() bool {
42: 	switch e {
43: 	case StartOver, Reimplement, RequestReview, Cancel, Reopen:
44: 		return true
45: 	}
46: 	return false
47: }
48: 
49: // transitionTable defines all valid state transitions.
50: // Key: current status → event → new status.
51: var transitionTable = map[Status]map[Event]Status{
52: 	StatusReady: {
53: 		PlanStart:      StatusPlanning,
54: 		ImplementStart: StatusImplementing,
55: 		Cancel:         StatusCancelled,
56: 	},
57: 	StatusPlanning: {
58: 		PlanStart:       StatusPlanning, // allow restart after crash/interrupt
59: 		PlannerFinished: StatusReady,
60: 		Cancel:          StatusCancelled,
61: 	},
62: 	StatusImplementing: {
63: 		ImplementFinished: StatusReviewing,
64: 		Cancel:            StatusCancelled,
65: 	},
66: 	StatusReviewing: {
67: 		ReviewApproved:         StatusDone,
68: 		ReviewChangesRequested: StatusImplementing,
69: 		Cancel:                 StatusCancelled,
70: 	},
71: 	StatusDone: {
72: 		StartOver:     StatusPlanning,
73: 		Reimplement:   StatusImplementing, // resume implementation without resetting branch
74: 		RequestReview: StatusReviewing,    // retrigger review for unmerged branches
75: 		Cancel:        StatusCancelled,    // explicit user cancellation from done
76: 	},
77: 	StatusCancelled: {
78: 		Reopen: StatusPlanning,
79: 	},
80: }
81: 
82: // ApplyTransition returns the new status for the given current status and event.
83: // Returns an error if the transition is not valid.
84: func ApplyTransition(current Status, event Event) (Status, error) {
85: 	events, ok := transitionTable[current]
86: 	if !ok {
87: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("no transitions defined for status %q", current)
88: 	}
89: 	next, ok := events[event]
90: 	if !ok {
91: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid transition: %q + %q", current, event)
92: 	}
93: 	return next, nil
94: }
95: 
96: // PlanStateMachine is the sole writer of plan state. All plan status mutations
97: // must flow through Transition(). The store handles concurrency via SQLite.
98: type PlanStateMachine struct {
99: 	dir     string          // docs/plans/ directory (for file operations)
100: 	store   planstore.Store // always non-nil
101: 	project string          // project name used with the store
102: }
103: 
104: // New creates a PlanStateMachine backed by the given store.
105: func New(store planstore.Store, project, dir string) *PlanStateMachine {
106: 	return &PlanStateMachine{dir: dir, store: store, project: project}
107: }
108: 
109: // Transition applies an event to a plan's current status. It reads the current
110: // state from the store, validates the transition, writes the new state, and returns.
111: // Concurrency is handled server-side via SQLite's own locking.
112: func (m *PlanStateMachine) Transition(planFile string, event Event) error {
113: 	ps, err := planstate.Load(m.store, m.project, m.dir)
114: 	if err != nil {
115: 		return fmt.Errorf("load plan state: %w", err)
116: 	}
117: 	entry, ok := ps.Entry(planFile)
118: 	if !ok {
119: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", planFile)
120: 	}
121: 	currentStatus := mapLegacyStatus(entry.Status)
122: 	newStatus, err := ApplyTransition(currentStatus, event)
123: 	if err != nil {
124: 		return err
125: 	}
126: 	// ForceSetStatus writes through to the store.
127: 	return ps.ForceSetStatus(planFile, planstate.Status(newStatus))
128: }
129: 
130: // mapLegacyStatus converts old planstate statuses to FSM statuses.
131: // Handles the consolidated aliases (in_progress → implementing, completed/finished → done).
132: func mapLegacyStatus(s planstate.Status) Status {
133: 	switch s {
134: 	case "in_progress":
135: 		return StatusImplementing
136: 	case "completed", "finished":
137: 		return StatusDone
138: 	default:
139: 		return Status(s)
140: 	}
141: }

(End of file - total 141 lines)
</content>

65: // the .md file on disk). The store is always required — there is no JSON fallback.
66: func Load(store planstore.Store, project, dir string) (*PlanState, error) {
