77: 	ps := &PlanState{
78: 		Dir:          dir,
79: 		Plans:        make(map[string]PlanEntry, len(plans)),
80: 		TopicEntries: make(map[string]TopicEntry, len(topics)),
81: 		store:        store,
82: 		project:      project,
83: 	}
84: 
85: 	for _, e := range plans {
86: 		ps.Plans[e.Filename] = PlanEntry{
87: 			Status:        Status(e.Status),
88: 			Description:   e.Description,
89: 			Branch:        e.Branch,
90: 			Topic:         e.Topic,
91: 			CreatedAt:     e.CreatedAt,
92: 			Implemented:   e.Implemented,
93: 			ClickUpTaskID: e.ClickUpTaskID,
94: 		}
95: 	}
96: 
97: 	for _, t := range topics {
98: 		ps.TopicEntries[t.Name] = TopicEntry{CreatedAt: t.CreatedAt}
99: 	}
100: 
101: 	return ps, nil
102: }
103: 
104: // Topics returns all topic entries sorted by name.
105: func (ps *PlanState) Topics() []TopicInfo {
106: 	// Discover topics from both TopicEntries and plan topic fields.
107: 	seen := make(map[string]TopicInfo)
108: 	for name, entry := range ps.TopicEntries {
109: 		seen[name] = TopicInfo{Name: name, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt}
110: 	}
111: 	for _, entry := range ps.Plans {
112: 		if entry.Topic != "" {
113: 			if _, ok := seen[entry.Topic]; !ok {
114: 				seen[entry.Topic] = TopicInfo{Name: entry.Topic}
115: 			}
116: 		}
117: 	}
118: 	result := make([]TopicInfo, 0, len(seen))
119: 	for _, info := range seen {
120: 		result = append(result, info)
121: 	}
122: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
123: 		return result[i].Name < result[j].Name
124: 	})
125: 	return result
126: }
127: 
128: // PlansByTopic returns all plans in the given topic, sorted by filename.
129: func (ps *PlanState) PlansByTopic(topic string) []PlanInfo {
130: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0)
131: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
132: 		if entry.Topic == topic {
133: 			result = append(result, PlanInfo{
134: 				Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
135: 				Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
136: 				Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
137: 			})
138: 		}
139: 	}
140: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
141: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
142: 	})
143: 	return result
144: }
145: 
146: // UngroupedPlans returns all active plans with no topic, sorted by filename.
147: func (ps *PlanState) UngroupedPlans() []PlanInfo {
148: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0)
149: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
150: 		if entry.Status == StatusDone || entry.Status == StatusCancelled {
151: 			continue
152: 		}
153: 		if entry.Topic == "" {
154: 			result = append(result, PlanInfo{
155: 				Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
156: 				Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
157: 				CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
158: 			})
159: 		}
160: 	}
161: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
162: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
163: 	})
164: 	return result
165: }
166: 
167: // hasTopicEntry returns true if the given topic name exists in TopicEntries.
168: func (ps *PlanState) hasTopicEntry(topic string) bool {
169: 	if ps.TopicEntries == nil {
170: 		return false
171: 	}
172: 	_, exists := ps.TopicEntries[topic]
173: 	return exists
174: }
175: 
176: // HasRunningCoderInTopic checks if any plan in the given topic (other than
177: // excludePlan) has status StatusImplementing. Returns the conflicting plan filename.
178: func (ps *PlanState) HasRunningCoderInTopic(topic, excludePlan string) (bool, string) {
179: 	if topic == "" {
180: 		return false, ""
181: 	}
182: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
183: 		if filename == excludePlan {
184: 			continue
185: 		}
186: 		if entry.Topic == topic && entry.Status == StatusImplementing {
187: 			return true, filename
188: 		}
189: 	}
190: 	return false, ""
191: }
192: 
193: // Unfinished returns plans that are not done or cancelled, sorted by filename.
194: func (ps *PlanState) Unfinished() []PlanInfo {
195: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0, len(ps.Plans))
196: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
197: 		if entry.Status == StatusDone || entry.Status == StatusCancelled {
198: 			continue
199: 		}
200: 		result = append(result, PlanInfo{
201: 			Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
202: 			Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
203: 			Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
204: 		})
205: 	}
206: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
207: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
208: 	})
209: 	return result
210: }
211: 
212: // Finished returns plans that are done, sorted by creation time (newest first).
213: func (ps *PlanState) Finished() []PlanInfo {
214: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0)
215: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
216: 		if entry.Status != StatusDone {
217: 			continue
218: 		}
219: 		result = append(result, PlanInfo{
220: 			Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
221: 			Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
222: 			Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
223: 		})
224: 	}
225: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
226: 		if !result[i].CreatedAt.Equal(result[j].CreatedAt) {
227: 			return result[i].CreatedAt.After(result[j].CreatedAt)
228: 		}
229: 		return result[i].Filename > result[j].Filename
230: 	})
231: 	return result
232: }
233: 
234: // Cancelled returns all cancelled plans, sorted by filename.
235: func (ps *PlanState) Cancelled() []PlanInfo {
236: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0)
237: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
238: 		if entry.Status != StatusCancelled {
239: 			continue
240: 		}
241: 		result = append(result, PlanInfo{
242: 			Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
243: 			Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
244: 			Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
245: 		})
246: 	}
247: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
248: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
249: 	})
250: 	return result
251: }
252: 
253: // List returns all plans (including done and cancelled), sorted by filename.
254: func (ps *PlanState) List() []PlanInfo {
255: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0, len(ps.Plans))
256: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
257: 		result = append(result, PlanInfo{
258: 			Filename:    filename,
259: 			Status:      entry.Status,
260: 			Description: entry.Description,
261: 			Branch:      entry.Branch,
262: 			Topic:       entry.Topic,
263: 			CreatedAt:   entry.CreatedAt,
264: 		})
265: 	}
266: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
267: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
268: 	})
269: 	return result
270: }
271: 
272: // IsDone returns true only if the given plan has status StatusDone.
273: func (ps *PlanState) IsDone(filename string) bool {
274: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
275: 	if !ok {
276: 		return false
277: 	}
278: 	return entry.Status == StatusDone
279: }
280: 
281: // ForceSetStatus overrides a plan's status regardless of FSM rules.
282: // Validates the status is a known value. Use only for manual overrides (e.g. kq plan set-status --force).
283: func (ps *PlanState) ForceSetStatus(filename string, status Status) error {
284: 	if !isValidStatus(status) {
285: 		return fmt.Errorf("invalid status %q: must be one of ready, planning, implementing, reviewing, done, cancelled", status)
286: 	}
287: 	if _, ok := ps.Plans[filename]; !ok {
288: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
289: 	}
290: 	entry := ps.Plans[filename]
291: 	entry.Status = status
292: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
293: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
294: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
295: 	}
296: 	return nil
297: }
298: 
299: // isValidStatus returns true if s is a recognised lifecycle status.
300: func isValidStatus(s Status) bool {
301: 	switch s {
302: 	case StatusReady, StatusPlanning, StatusImplementing, StatusReviewing, StatusDone, StatusCancelled:
303: 		return true
304: 	}
305: 	return false
306: }
307: 
308: // setStatus updates a plan's status and persists to the store.
309: // Unexported: only for use within this package (tests). Production code must use planfsm.Transition.
310: func (ps *PlanState) setStatus(filename string, status Status) error {
311: 	if ps.Plans == nil {
312: 		ps.Plans = make(map[string]PlanEntry)
313: 	}
314: 	entry := ps.Plans[filename]
315: 	entry.Status = status
316: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
317: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
318: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
319: 	}
320: 	return nil
321: }
322: 
323: // CreateWithContent adds a new plan entry with markdown content stored in the backend.
324: // The plan entry is created with StatusReady, and the content is persisted via store.SetContent.
325: // Returns an error if the plan already exists.
326: func (ps *PlanState) CreateWithContent(filename, description, branch, topic string, createdAt time.Time, content string) error {
327: 	if err := ps.Create(filename, description, branch, topic, createdAt); err != nil {
328: 		return err
329: 	}
330: 	if err := ps.store.SetContent(ps.project, filename, content); err != nil {
331: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store set content: %w", err)
332: 	}
333: 	return nil
334: }
335: 
336: // GetContent retrieves the markdown content for the given plan filename from the store.
337: func (ps *PlanState) GetContent(filename string) (string, error) {
338: 	return ps.store.GetContent(ps.project, filename)
339: }
340: 
341: // SetContent updates the markdown content for the given plan filename in the store.
342: func (ps *PlanState) SetContent(filename, content string) error {
343: 	return ps.store.SetContent(ps.project, filename, content)
344: }
345: 
346: // Create adds a new plan entry to the state and auto-creates the topic if needed.
347: func (ps *PlanState) Create(filename, description, branch, topic string, createdAt time.Time) error {
348: 	if ps.Plans == nil {
349: 		ps.Plans = make(map[string]PlanEntry)
350: 	}
351: 	if _, exists := ps.Plans[filename]; exists {
352: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan already exists: %s", filename)
353: 	}
354: 	entry := PlanEntry{
355: 		Status:      StatusReady,
356: 		Description: description,
357: 		Branch:      branch,
358: 		Topic:       topic,
359: 		CreatedAt:   createdAt.UTC(),
360: 	}
361: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
362: 	// Auto-create topic entry if it doesn't exist
363: 	if topic != "" {
364: 		if ps.TopicEntries == nil {
365: 			ps.TopicEntries = make(map[string]TopicEntry)
366: 		}
367: 		if _, exists := ps.TopicEntries[topic]; !exists {
368: 			ps.TopicEntries[topic] = TopicEntry{CreatedAt: createdAt.UTC()}
369: 		}
370: 	}
371: 	if err := ps.store.Create(ps.project, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
372: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
373: 	}
374: 	// Auto-create topic in store if needed
375: 	if topic != "" {
376: 		topicEntry := planstore.TopicEntry{Name: topic, CreatedAt: createdAt.UTC()}
377: 		if err := ps.store.CreateTopic(ps.project, topicEntry); err != nil {
378: 			// Ignore "already exists" errors for topics
379: 			if !isAlreadyExistsError(err) {
380: 				return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
381: 			}
382: 		}
383: 	}
384: 	return nil
385: }
386: 
387: // Register adds a new plan entry with metadata and persists to the store.
388: // Returns an error if the plan already exists.
389: func (ps *PlanState) Register(filename, description, branch string, createdAt time.Time) error {
390: 	if ps.Plans == nil {
391: 		ps.Plans = make(map[string]PlanEntry)
392: 	}
393: 	if _, exists := ps.Plans[filename]; exists {
394: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan already exists: %s", filename)
395: 	}
396: 	entry := PlanEntry{
397: 		Status:      StatusReady,
398: 		Description: description,
399: 		Branch:      branch,
400: 		CreatedAt:   createdAt.UTC(),
401: 	}
402: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
403: 	if err := ps.store.Create(ps.project, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
404: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
405: 	}
406: 	return nil
407: }
408: 
409: // Entry returns the PlanEntry for the given filename, and whether it exists.
410: func (ps *PlanState) Entry(filename string) (PlanEntry, bool) {
411: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
412: 	return entry, ok
413: }
414: 
415: // SetTopic assigns a topic to an existing plan entry and persists to the store.
416: // If topic is non-empty and does not yet exist in TopicEntries, it is auto-created.
417: // Pass an empty string to remove the plan from any topic.
418: func (ps *PlanState) SetTopic(filename, topic string) error {
419: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
420: 	if !ok {
421: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
422: 	}
423: 	entry.Topic = topic
424: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
425: 	// Auto-create topic entry if it doesn't exist
426: 	if topic != "" {
427: 		if ps.TopicEntries == nil {
428: 			ps.TopicEntries = make(map[string]TopicEntry)
429: 		}
430: 		if _, exists := ps.TopicEntries[topic]; !exists {
431: 			ps.TopicEntries[topic] = TopicEntry{CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC()}
432: 		}
433: 	}
434: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
435: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
436: 	}
437: 	// Auto-create topic in store if needed
438: 	if topic != "" {
439: 		topicEntry := planstore.TopicEntry{Name: topic, CreatedAt: ps.TopicEntries[topic].CreatedAt}
440: 		if err := ps.store.CreateTopic(ps.project, topicEntry); err != nil {
441: 			if !isAlreadyExistsError(err) {
442: 				return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
443: 			}
444: 		}
445: 	}
446: 	return nil
447: }
448: 
449: // SetBranch assigns a branch name to an existing plan entry and persists to the store.
450: func (ps *PlanState) SetBranch(filename, branch string) error {
451: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
452: 	if !ok {
453: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
454: 	}
455: 	entry.Branch = branch
456: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
457: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
458: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
459: 	}
460: 	return nil
461: }
462: 
463: // Save is a no-op — all mutations write through to the store immediately.
464: // Retained for API compatibility.
465: func (ps *PlanState) Save() error {
466: 	return nil
467: }
468: 
469: // DisplayName strips the date prefix and .md extension from a plan filename.
470: // "2026-02-20-my-feature.md" → "my-feature"
471: // "plain-plan.md" → "plain-plan"
472: func DisplayName(filename string) string {
473: 	name := strings.TrimSuffix(filename, ".md")
474: 	if len(name) > 11 && name[4] == '-' && name[7] == '-' && name[10] == '-' {
475: 		name = name[11:]
476: 	}
477: 	return name
478: }
479: 
480: // Rename renames a plan by giving it a new display name slug.
481: // It renames the .md file on disk (if it exists), rekeys the planstate entry,
482: // and persists the updated state. The date prefix of the old filename is preserved.
483: // newName should be a human-readable name (e.g., "auth refactor") which will be
484: // slugified automatically. Returns the new filename on success.
485: func (ps *PlanState) Rename(oldFilename, newName string) (string, error) {
486: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[oldFilename]
487: 	if !ok {
488: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", oldFilename)
489: 	}
490: 	if newName == "" {
491: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("new name cannot be empty")
492: 	}
493: 
494: 	// Build new filename preserving the date prefix (YYYY-MM-DD-) if present.
495: 	newSlug := slugify(newName)
496: 	if newSlug == "" {
497: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("new name produced an empty slug")
498: 	}
499: 	var newFilename string
500: 	if len(oldFilename) > 11 && oldFilename[4] == '-' && oldFilename[7] == '-' && oldFilename[10] == '-' {
501: 		newFilename = oldFilename[:11] + newSlug + ".md"
502: 	} else {
503: 		newFilename = newSlug + ".md"
504: 	}
505: 
506: 	if newFilename == oldFilename {
507: 		return oldFilename, nil // nothing to do
508: 	}
509: 	if _, exists := ps.Plans[newFilename]; exists {
510: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("a plan named %q already exists", newFilename)
511: 	}
512: 
513: 	// Rename file on disk if it exists.
514: 	oldPath := filepath.Join(ps.Dir, oldFilename)
515: 	newPath := filepath.Join(ps.Dir, newFilename)
516: 	if _, err := os.Stat(oldPath); err == nil {
517: 		if err := os.Rename(oldPath, newPath); err != nil {
518: 			return "", fmt.Errorf("rename plan file: %w", err)
519: 		}
520: 	}
521: 
522: 	// Rekey the planstate entry.
523: 	ps.Plans[newFilename] = entry
524: 	delete(ps.Plans, oldFilename)
525: 
526: 	if err := ps.store.Rename(ps.project, oldFilename, newFilename); err != nil {
527: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
528: 	}
529: 	return newFilename, nil
530: }
531: 
532: // slugify converts a human name to a lowercase, hyphen-separated slug.
533: // "My Cool Feature!" → "my-cool-feature"
534: func slugify(name string) string {
535: 	name = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(name))
536: 	// Replace any sequence of non-alphanumeric characters with a hyphen.
537: 	result := make([]rune, 0, len(name))
538: 	inHyphen := false
539: 	for _, r := range name {
540: 		if r >= 'a' && r <= 'z' || r >= '0' && r <= '9' {
541: 			result = append(result, r)
542: 			inHyphen = false
543: 		} else if !inHyphen && len(result) > 0 {
544: 			result = append(result, '-')
545: 			inHyphen = true
546: 		}
547: 	}
548: 	// Trim trailing hyphen.
549: 	for len(result) > 0 && result[len(result)-1] == '-' {
550: 		result = result[:len(result)-1]
551: 	}
552: 	return string(result)
553: }
554: 
555: // isAlreadyExistsError returns true if the error indicates a duplicate resource.
556: func isAlreadyExistsError(err error) bool {
557: 	if err == nil {
558: 		return false
559: 	}
560: 	msg := err.Error()
561: 	return strings.Contains(msg, "already exists")
562: }
563: 
564: // toPlanstoreEntry converts a local PlanEntry to a planstore.PlanEntry for
565: // writing to the store.
566: func (ps *PlanState) toPlanstoreEntry(filename string, e PlanEntry) planstore.PlanEntry {
567: 	return planstore.PlanEntry{
568: 		Filename:      filename,
569: 		Status:        planstore.Status(e.Status),
570: 		Description:   e.Description,
571: 		Branch:        e.Branch,
572: 		Topic:         e.Topic,
573: 		CreatedAt:     e.CreatedAt,
574: 		Implemented:   e.Implemented,
575: 		ClickUpTaskID: e.ClickUpTaskID,
576: 	}
577: }
578: 
579: // SetClickUpTaskID assigns a ClickUp task ID to an existing plan entry and
580: // persists to the store.
581: func (ps *PlanState) SetClickUpTaskID(filename, taskID string) error {
582: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
583: 	if !ok {
584: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
585: 	}
586: 	entry.ClickUpTaskID = taskID
587: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
588: 	if err := ps.store.SetClickUpTaskID(ps.project, filename, taskID); err != nil {
589: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
590: 	}
591: 	return nil
592: }

<content>1: // Package planstore provides a Store interface for plan state persistence,
2: // with a SQLite implementation for direct DB access and an HTTP implementation
3: // for client-server communication.
4: package planstore
5: 
6: import "time"
7: 
8: // Status represents the lifecycle state of a plan.
9: // These constants mirror planstate.Status to keep planstore self-contained
10: // and avoid circular imports.
11: type Status string
12: 
13: const (
14: 	StatusReady        Status = "ready"
15: 	StatusDone         Status = "done"
16: 	StatusReviewing    Status = "reviewing"
17: 	StatusCancelled    Status = "cancelled"
18: 	StatusPlanning     Status = "planning"
19: 	StatusImplementing Status = "implementing"
20: )
21: 
22: // PlanEntry holds the persisted metadata for a single plan.
23: type PlanEntry struct {
24: 	Filename      string    `json:"filename"`
25: 	Status        Status    `json:"status"`
26: 	Description   string    `json:"description,omitempty"`
27: 	Branch        string    `json:"branch,omitempty"`
28: 	Topic         string    `json:"topic,omitempty"`
29: 	CreatedAt     time.Time `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
30: 	Implemented   string    `json:"implemented,omitempty"`
31: 	Content       string    `json:"content,omitempty"`
32: 	ClickUpTaskID string    `json:"clickup_task_id,omitempty"`
33: }
34: 
35: // TopicEntry holds the persisted metadata for a topic grouping.
36: type TopicEntry struct {
37: 	Name      string    `json:"name"`
38: 	CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
39: }
40: 
41: // Store is the interface for plan state persistence. Implementations include
42: // SQLiteStore (direct DB access, used by the server) and HTTPStore (client
43: // that talks to the server over HTTP).
44: type Store interface {
45: 	// Plan CRUD
46: 	Create(project string, entry PlanEntry) error
47: 	Get(project, filename string) (PlanEntry, error)
48: 	Update(project, filename string, entry PlanEntry) error
49: 	Rename(project, oldFilename, newFilename string) error
50: 
51: 	// Content access
52: 	GetContent(project, filename string) (string, error)

state.output:
<path>/home/kas/dev/kasmos/internal/opencodesession/title.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: // Package opencodesession manages opencode session titles for kasmos-spawned agents.
2: package opencodesession
3: 
4: import (
5: 	"database/sql"
6: 	"fmt"
7: 	"os"
8: 	"path/filepath"
9: 	"time"
10: 
11: 	_ "modernc.org/sqlite" // register sqlite driver
12: )
13: 
14: // kasPrefix is the prefix applied to all kasmos-managed session titles.
15: // It distinguishes our titles from opencode's auto-generated ones.
16: const kasPrefix = "kas: "
17: 
18: // TitleOpts holds the metadata used to build a human-readable session title.
19: type TitleOpts struct {
20: 	// PlanName is the plan slug (e.g. "automatic-session-naming").
21: 	PlanName string
22: 	// AgentType is the role of the agent (e.g. "planner", "coder", "reviewer", "fixer").
23: 	AgentType string
24: 	// WaveNumber and TaskNumber, when non-zero, append wave/task context to the title.
25: 	WaveNumber int
26: 	TaskNumber int
27: 	// InstanceTitle is used as the subject for ad-hoc sessions or when PlanName is empty.
28: 	InstanceTitle string
29: }
30: 
31: // agentVerbs maps agent type identifiers to their title verb prefix.
32: var agentVerbs = map[string]string{
33: 	"planner":  "plan",
34: 	"coder":    "implement",
35: 	"reviewer": "review",
36: 	"fixer":    "fix",
37: }
38: 
39: // BuildTitle returns a kasmos-managed title string for an opencode session.
40: // All titles are prefixed with "kas: " to distinguish them from opencode's
41: // auto-generated titles.
42: func BuildTitle(opts TitleOpts) string {
43: 	// Determine the subject — prefer PlanName, fall back to InstanceTitle.
44: 	subject := opts.PlanName
45: 	if subject == "" {
46: 		subject = opts.InstanceTitle
47: 	}
48: 
49: 	// Look up the verb for the agent type.
50: 	verb, hasVerb := agentVerbs[opts.AgentType]
51: 
52: 	// Ad-hoc session with no recognized agent type: just prefix the subject.
53: 	if !hasVerb {
54: 		return kasPrefix + subject
55: 	}
56: 
57: 	title := fmt.Sprintf("%s%s %s", kasPrefix, verb, subject)
58: 
59: 	// Append wave/task context when both are specified.
60: 	if opts.WaveNumber > 0 && opts.TaskNumber > 0 {
61: 		title += fmt.Sprintf(" w%d/t%d", opts.WaveNumber, opts.TaskNumber)
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	return title
65: }
66: 
67: // ClaimAndSetTitle atomically claims the first unclaimed opencode session that
68: // matches the given working directory and was created after beforeStart, then
69: // sets its title to the provided value.
70: //
71: // "Unclaimed" means the title does NOT start with "kas: ". This prevents two
72: // concurrent goroutines from claiming the same session in a parallel wave.
73: //
74: // It is a best-effort operation: if no matching session is found, it returns nil.
75: func ClaimAndSetTitle(db *sql.DB, workDir string, beforeStart time.Time, title string) error {
76: 	beforeStartMs := beforeStart.UnixMilli()
77: 
78: 	// Find all unclaimed sessions in this directory created after beforeStart.
79: 	rows, err := db.Query(
80: 		`SELECT id FROM session
81: 		 WHERE directory = ? AND time_created >= ? AND title NOT LIKE 'kas: %'
82: 		 ORDER BY time_created ASC`,
83: 		workDir, beforeStartMs,
84: 	)
85: 	if err != nil {
86: 		return fmt.Errorf("opencodesession: query sessions: %w", err)
87: 	}
88: 	defer rows.Close()
89: 
90: 	var candidates []string
91: 	for rows.Next() {
92: 		var id string
93: 		if err := rows.Scan(&id); err != nil {
94: 			return fmt.Errorf("opencodesession: scan session id: %w", err)
95: 		}
96: 		candidates = append(candidates, id)
97: 	}
98: 	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
99: 		return fmt.Errorf("opencodesession: iterate sessions: %w", err)
100: 	}
101: 	rows.Close() // Close before attempting updates.
102: 
103: 	nowMs := time.Now().UnixMilli()
104: 
105: 	// Attempt an atomic claim on each candidate in ascending time_created order.
106: 	for _, id := range candidates {
107: 		res, err := db.Exec(
108: 			`UPDATE session SET title = ?, time_updated = ?
109: 			 WHERE id = ? AND title NOT LIKE 'kas: %'`,
110: 			title, nowMs, id,
111: 		)
112: 		if err != nil {
113: 			return fmt.Errorf("opencodesession: update session title: %w", err)
114: 		}
115: 		affected, err := res.RowsAffected()
116: 		if err != nil {
117: 			return fmt.Errorf("opencodesession: rows affected: %w", err)
118: 		}
119: 		if affected == 1 {
120: 			// Successfully claimed this session.
121: 			return nil
122: 		}
123: 		// Another goroutine claimed this candidate; try the next one.
124: 	}
125: 
126: 	// No candidates remain — best-effort, not an error.
127: 	return nil
128: }
129: 
130: // resolveDBPath returns the path to the opencode SQLite database.
131: func resolveDBPath() string {
132: 	if xdg := os.Getenv("XDG_DATA_HOME"); xdg != "" {
133: 		return filepath.Join(xdg, "opencode", "opencode.db")
134: 	}
135: 	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
136: 	if err != nil {
137: 		// Fall back to a relative path as last resort.
138: 		return filepath.Join(".local", "share", "opencode", "opencode.db")
139: 	}
140: 	return filepath.Join(home, ".local", "share", "opencode", "opencode.db")
141: }
142: 
143: // SetTitleDirect opens the opencode SQLite DB and claims+sets the given
144: // pre-built title on the matching session. Use this when the title has already
145: // been constructed (e.g. via BuildTitle) and you only need the DB write.
146: //
147: // DB path resolution order:
148: //  1. $XDG_DATA_HOME/opencode/opencode.db
149: //  2. ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db
150: func SetTitleDirect(workDir string, beforeStart time.Time, title string) error {
151: 	dbPath := resolveDBPath()
152: 	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dbPath+"?_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)")
153: 	if err != nil {
154: 		return fmt.Errorf("opencodesession: open db %s: %w", dbPath, err)
155: 	}
156: 	defer db.Close()
157: 	return ClaimAndSetTitle(db, workDir, beforeStart, title)
158: }

(End of file - total 158 lines)
</content>

parts.text:
Now I have complete understanding. Let me write the plan. The key insight is:

1. **No review cycle count exists anywhere** — the `PlanEntry` has no cycle counter
2. **Instance titles are hardcoded** — `planName + "-review"` and `planName + "-implement"` in `spawnReviewer()` and `spawnCoderWithFeedback()`
3. **Tmux session names derive from instance titles** — `toKasTmuxName(title)` → `kas_<title>`
4. **The clash happens** because cycle 2 creates instances with the same title as cycle 1 (which was just paused/killed)

The fix needs:
- A `ReviewCycleCount` field on `PlanEntry` (persisted in DB)
- Increment it on `ReviewChangesRequested` 
- Use it to suffix instance titles: `-review-1`, `-fix-1`, `-review-2`, `-fix-2`
- The opencode session title should also reflect the cycle

93: }

95: // migrateAddColumn adds a column to the plans table if it doesn't already
96: // exist, running the provided ALTER TABLE statement when needed.
97: func migrateAddColumn(db *sql.DB, columnName, alterSQL string) error {
98: 	rows, err := db.Query("PRAGMA table_info(plans)")
99: 	if err != nil {
100: 		return fmt.Errorf("query table info: %w", err)
101: 	}
102: 	defer rows.Close()
103: 
104: 	for rows.Next() {
105: 		var cid int
106: 		var name, colType string
107: 		var notNull int
108: 		var dfltValue sql.NullString
109: 		var pk int
110: 		if err := rows.Scan(&cid, &name, &colType, &notNull, &dfltValue, &pk); err != nil {
111: 			return fmt.Errorf("scan table info: %w", err)
112: 		}
113: 		if name == columnName {
114: 			return nil // column already exists
115: 		}
116: 	}
117: 	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
118: 		return fmt.Errorf("iterate table info: %w", err)
119: 	}
120: 
121: 	// Column doesn't exist — add it.
122: 	if _, err := db.Exec(alterSQL); err != nil {
123: 		return fmt.Errorf("add %s column: %w", columnName, err)
124: 	}
125: 	return nil
126: }

input.content:
# Review Cycle Count in Instance Names Implementation Plan

**Goal:** Include review cycle count in all instance names, tmux session names, and opencode session titles so that each review/fix cycle gets unique identifiers (`-review-1`, `-fix-1`, `-review-2`, `-fix-2`, etc.), preventing clashes with existing sessions, caches, and worktree state.

**Architecture:** Add a `ReviewCycleCount` field to `PlanEntry` (persisted in the planstore SQLite DB). Increment it each time `ReviewChangesRequested` fires. The cycle count flows into instance title construction in `spawnReviewer()`, `spawnCoderWithFeedback()`, and the opencode session title builder. The initial review/implement cycle uses `-review-1`/`-fix-1` (not bare `-review`/`-implement`) so naming is consistent from the start.

**Tech Stack:** Go, SQLite (planstore), bubbletea TUI (app package), tmux session management

**Size:** Medium (estimated ~3 hours, 4 tasks, 2 waves)

---

## Wave 1: Persist Review Cycle Count in Plan Store

### Task 1: Add ReviewCycleCount to planstore, planstate, and DB migration

**Files:**
- Modify: `config/planstore/store.go`
- Modify: `config/planstore/sqlite.go`
- Modify: `config/planstore/http.go`
- Modify: `config/planstore/server.go`
- Modify: `config/planstate/planstate.go`
- Test: `config/planstore/sqlite_test.go`
- Test: `config/planstate/planstate_test.go`

**Step 1: write the failing test**

Add a test in `config/planstore/sqlite_test.go` that creates a plan, calls `SetReviewCycleCount(project, filename, 2)`, reloads, and asserts the count is persisted. Also add a test in `config/planstate/planstate_test.go` that verifies `IncrementReviewCycleCount` bumps the count and persists it.

```go
func TestSQLiteStore_SetReviewCycleCount(t *testing.T) {
    store := newTestStore(t)
    entry := planstore.PlanEntry{Filename: "test.md", Status: "ready"}
    require.NoError(t, store.Create("proj", entry))

    require.NoError(t, store.SetReviewCycleCount("proj", "test.md", 2))

    got, err := store.Get("proj", "test.md")
    require.NoError(t, err)
    assert.Equal(t, 2, got.ReviewCycleCount)
}
```

**Step 2: run test to verify it fails**

```bash
go test ./config/planstore/... -run TestSQLiteStore_SetReviewCycleCount -v
```

expected: FAIL — `SetReviewCycleCount` undefined, `ReviewCycleCount` field missing

**Step 3: write minimal implementation**

1. **`config/planstore/store.go`**: Add `ReviewCycleCount int` field to `PlanEntry`. Add `SetReviewCycleCount(project, filename string, count int) error` to the `Store` interface.

2. **`config/planstore/sqlite.go`**:
   - Add migration constant: `const reviewCycleCountMigration = "ALTER TABLE plans ADD COLUMN review_cycle_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0"`
   - Call `migrateAddColumn(db, "review_cycle_count", reviewCycleCountMigration)` in `NewSQLiteStore`.
   - Add `review_cycle_count` to all SELECT column lists, INSERT, and UPDATE queries.
   - Update `scanPlanEntry` and `scanPlanEntries` to scan the new column.
   - Implement `SetReviewCycleCount` method (targeted UPDATE, same pattern as `SetClickUpTaskID`).

3. **`config/planstore/http.go`**: Implement `SetReviewCycleCount` on `HTTPStore` — POST to the server endpoint with the count.

4. **`config/planstore/server.go`**: Add handler for `SetReviewCycleCount` requests (same pattern as ClickUp task ID handler).

5. **`config/planstate/planstate.go`**:
   - Add `ReviewCycleCount int` to `PlanEntry`.
   - Map it in `Load()` from `planstore.PlanEntry.ReviewCycleCount`.
   - Map it in `toPlanstoreEntry()`.
   - Add `IncrementReviewCycleCount(filename string) (int, error)` method that bumps the count by 1, persists, and returns the new value.
   - Add `ReviewCycleCount(filename string) int` getter that returns 0 if plan not found.

**Step 4: run test to verify it passes**

```bash
go test ./config/planstore/... -run TestSQLiteStore_SetReviewCycleCount -v
go test ./config/planstate/... -v
```

expected: PASS

**Step 5: commit**

```bash
git add config/planstore/ config/planstate/
git commit -m "feat: add ReviewCycleCount to planstore and planstate"
```

---

## Wave 2: Use Cycle Count in Instance Naming

> **depends on wave 1:** requires `ReviewCycleCount` field and `IncrementReviewCycleCount` method from planstate

### Task 2: Suffix review/fix instance titles with cycle count

**Files:**
- Modify: `app/app_state.go` (functions: `spawnReviewer`, `spawnCoderWithFeedback`, `spawnPlanAgent`)
- Modify: `app/app.go` (signal handler for `ReviewChangesRequested`)
- Test: `app/app_plan_completion_test.go`

**Step 1: write the failing test**

Add a test `TestReviewCycleCount_InstanceNaming` that:
1. Creates a plan with `ReviewCycleCount: 0` (initial state).
2. Triggers the first review spawn and asserts the reviewer title is `<planName>-review-1`.
3. Simulates `ReviewChangesRequested` → asserts the fix-coder title is `<planName>-fix-1`.
4. Simulates the fix-coder completing → asserts the second reviewer title is `<planName>-review-2`.

```go
func TestReviewCycleCount_InstanceNaming(t *testing.T) {
    // ... setup plan with ReviewCycleCount 0 ...
    // After spawnReviewer: reviewer title should be "<name>-review-1"
    // After ReviewChangesRequested: coder title should be "<name>-fix-1"
    // After second spawnReviewer: reviewer title should be "<name>-review-2"
}
```

**Step 2: run test to verify it fails**

```bash
go test ./app/... -run TestReviewCycleCount_InstanceNaming -v
```

expected: FAIL — titles still use bare `-review` / `-implement`

**Step 3: write minimal implementation**

1. **`app/app_state.go` — `spawnReviewer()`**:
   - Before creating the instance, read the current cycle count: `cycle := m.planState.ReviewCycleCount(planFile) + 1`
   - Increment and persist: call `m.planState.IncrementReviewCycleCount(planFile)` (which returns the new count)
   - Change title from `planName + "-review"` to `fmt.Sprintf("%s-review-%d", planName, cycle)`

2. **`app/app_state.go` — `spawnCoderWithFeedback()`**:
   - Read the current cycle count: `cycle := m.planState.ReviewCycleCount(planFile)` (already incremented by the ReviewChangesRequested handler)
   - Change title from `planName + "-implement"` to `fmt.Sprintf("%s-fix-%d", planName, cycle)`

3. **`app/app.go` — `ReviewChangesRequested` signal handler**: No change needed — the cycle count is incremented by `spawnReviewer` when it creates the review session. The `spawnCoderWithFeedback` reads the current count.

   Actually, the flow is: reviewer writes `review-changes` sentinel → `ReviewChangesRequested` fires → `spawnCoderWithFeedback` is called. The cycle was already incremented when the reviewer was spawned. So `spawnCoderWithFeedback` just reads the current count.

4. **`app/app_state.go` — `spawnPlanAgent()`**: For the initial `"review"` action (user-triggered from context menu), also use the cycle-counted title. Read cycle count, increment, use `fmt.Sprintf("%s-review-%d", planName, cycle)`. For `"implement"` action, keep the existing title (initial implementation is not a fix cycle).

5. **Update `app/app_state.go` — `executePlanStage()` review case**: The review case calls `spawnPlanAgent(planFile, "review", ...)` which will now use the cycle-counted title.

**Step 4: run test to verify it passes**

```bash
go test ./app/... -run TestReviewCycleCount_InstanceNaming -v
```

expected: PASS

**Step 5: commit**

```bash
git add app/app_state.go app/app.go app/app_plan_completion_test.go
git commit -m "feat: suffix review/fix instance titles with cycle count"
```

### Task 3: Update opencode session title to include cycle count

**Files:**
- Modify: `internal/opencodesession/title.go`
- Modify: `session/instance_lifecycle.go` (function: `buildTitleOpts`)
- Modify: `session/instance.go` (add `ReviewCycleCount` field to `Instance`)
- Test: `internal/opencodesession/title_test.go`
- Test: `session/instance_title_test.go`

**Step 1: write the failing test**

Add test cases to `internal/opencodesession/title_test.go`:

```go
{
    name: "reviewer with cycle count",
    opts: TitleOpts{PlanName: "my-feature", AgentType: "reviewer", ReviewCycle: 2},
    want: "kas: review my-feature #2",
},
{
    name: "fixer with cycle count",
    opts: TitleOpts{PlanName: "my-feature", AgentType: "fixer", ReviewCycle: 1},
    want: "kas: fix my-feature #1",
},
```

And in `session/instance_title_test.go`, update the reviewer test case to include the cycle count.

**Step 2: run test to verify it fails**

```bash
go test ./internal/opencodesession/... -run TestBuildTitle -v
go test ./session/... -run TestBuildTitleOptsFromInstance -v
```

expected: FAIL — `ReviewCycle` field doesn't exist on `TitleOpts`

**Step 3: write minimal implementation**

1. **`internal/opencodesession/title.go`**:
   - Add `ReviewCycle int` to `TitleOpts`.
   - In `BuildTitle`, after constructing the base title, if `opts.ReviewCycle > 0`, append ` #N` to the title (e.g. `"kas: review my-feature #2"`).

2. **`session/instance.go`**: No new field needed on `Instance` — the cycle count is only needed at spawn time for the title string, and the title is already set by the caller. However, for the opencode session title (which is set later via `buildTitleOpts`), we need to pass the cycle count through. Add `ReviewCycleCount int` field to `Instance` and `InstanceOptions`.

3. **`session/instance_lifecycle.go` — `buildTitleOpts()`**: Pass `inst.ReviewCycleCount` as `ReviewCycle` in the `TitleOpts`.

4. **`app/app_state.go`**: When creating reviewer/fixer instances in `spawnReviewer` and `spawnCoderWithFeedback`, set `ReviewCycleCount` on the instance (it's already computed for the title).

**Step 4: run test to verify it passes**

```bash
go test ./internal/opencodesession/... -v
go test ./session/... -run TestBuildTitleOptsFromInstance -v
```

expected: PASS

**Step 5: commit**

```bash
git add internal/opencodesession/ session/ app/app_state.go
git commit -m "feat: include review cycle count in opencode session titles"
```

### Task 4: Update existing tests and fix serialization round-trip

**Files:**
- Modify: `app/app_plan_completion_test.go`
- Modify: `app/app_wave_orchestration_flow_test.go`
- Modify: `app/clickup_progress_test.go`
- Modify: `session/instance.go` (add `ReviewCycleCount` to `ToInstanceData`/`FromInstanceData`)
- Modify: `session/storage.go` (if `InstanceData` needs the field)
- Test: `app/app_plan_completion_test.go`

**Step 1: write the failing test**

Run the full test suite to identify all tests that assert on instance titles containing `-review` or `-implement` that now need updating to `-review-N` or `-fix-N` format.

```bash
go test ./... 2>&1 | head -100
```

**Step 2: run test to verify it fails**

```bash
go test ./app/... -v -count=1
```

expected: FAIL — existing tests assert on old title format (`"feature-review"` instead of `"feature-review-1"`)

**Step 3: write minimal implementation**

1. Update all test assertions that check for `-review` titles to use `-review-1` (or the appropriate cycle number).
2. Update test assertions that check for `-implement` titles in the review-feedback flow to use `-fix-1`.
3. Add `ReviewCycleCount` to `InstanceData` struct in `session/storage.go` and wire it through `ToInstanceData`/`FromInstanceData` for persistence across TUI restarts.
4. Ensure the `InstanceOptions` struct passes `ReviewCycleCount` through `NewInstance`.

**Step 4: run test to verify it passes**

```bash
go test ./... -count=1
```

expected: PASS — all tests pass with updated title format

**Step 5: commit**

```bash
git add app/ session/
git commit -m "fix: update tests and serialization for review cycle count"
```

metadata.preview:
# Review Cycle Count in Instance Names Implementation Plan

**Goal:** Include review cycle count in all instance names, tmux session names, and opencode session titles so that each review/fix cycle gets unique identifiers (`-review-1`, `-fix-1`, `-review-2`, `-fix-2`, etc.), preventing clashes with existing sessions, caches, and worktree state.

**Architecture:** Add a `ReviewCycleCount` field to `PlanEntry` (persisted in the planstore SQLite DB). Increment it each time `ReviewChangesRequested` fires. The cycle count flows into instance title construction in `spawnReviewer()`, `spawnCoderWithFeedback()`, and the opencode session title builder. The initial review/implement cycle uses `-review-1`/`-fix-1` (not bare `-review`/`-implement`) so naming is consistent from the start.

**Tech Stack:** Go, SQLite (planstore), bubbletea TUI (app package), tmux session management

**Size:** Medium (estimated ~3 hours, 4 tasks, 2 waves)

---

## Wave 1: Persist Review Cycle Count in Plan Store

### Task 1: Add ReviewCycleCount to planstore, planstate, and DB migration

**Files:**
- Modify: `config/planstore/store.go`
- Modify: `config/planstore/sqlite.go`
- Modify: `config/planstore/http.go`

input.content:
# Review Cycle Count in Instance Names Implementation Plan

**Goal:** Include review cycle count in all instance names, tmux session names, and opencode session titles so that each review/fix cycle gets unique identifiers (`-review-1`, `-fix-1`, `-review-2`, `-fix-2`, etc.), preventing clashes with existing sessions, caches, and worktree state.

**Architecture:** Add a `ReviewCycleCount` field to `PlanEntry` (persisted in the planstore SQLite DB). Increment it each time a reviewer is spawned (in `spawnReviewer`). The cycle count flows into instance title construction in `spawnReviewer()`, `spawnCoderWithFeedback()`, `spawnPlanAgent()`, and the opencode session title builder. The initial review cycle uses `-review-1`/`-fix-1` (not bare `-review`/`-implement`) so naming is consistent from the start. The fix-coder reads the current count (already incremented by the preceding reviewer spawn).

**Tech Stack:** Go, SQLite (planstore), bubbletea TUI (app package), tmux session management

**Size:** Medium (estimated ~3 hours, 3 tasks, 2 waves)

---

## Wave 1: Persist Review Cycle Count in Plan Store

### Task 1: Add ReviewCycleCount to planstore, planstate, and DB migration

**Files:**
- Modify: `config/planstore/store.go`
- Modify: `config/planstore/sqlite.go`
- Modify: `config/planstore/http.go`
- Modify: `config/planstore/server.go`
- Modify: `config/planstate/planstate.go`
- Test: `config/planstore/sqlite_test.go`
- Test: `config/planstate/planstate_test.go`

**Step 1: write the failing test**

Add a test in `config/planstore/sqlite_test.go` that creates a plan, calls `SetReviewCycleCount(project, filename, 2)`, reloads, and asserts the count is persisted. Also add a test in `config/planstate/planstate_test.go` that verifies `IncrementReviewCycleCount` bumps the count and persists it.

```go
func TestSQLiteStore_SetReviewCycleCount(t *testing.T) {
    store := newTestStore(t)
    entry := planstore.PlanEntry{Filename: "test.md", Status: "ready"}
    require.NoError(t, store.Create("proj", entry))

    require.NoError(t, store.SetReviewCycleCount("proj", "test.md", 2))

    got, err := store.Get("proj", "test.md")
    require.NoError(t, err)
    assert.Equal(t, 2, got.ReviewCycleCount)
}
```

**Step 2: run test to verify it fails**

```bash
go test ./config/planstore/... -run TestSQLiteStore_SetReviewCycleCount -v
```

expected: FAIL — `SetReviewCycleCount` undefined, `ReviewCycleCount` field missing

**Step 3: write minimal implementation**

1. **`config/planstore/store.go`**: Add `ReviewCycleCount int` field to `PlanEntry` (json tag: `"review_cycle_count,omitempty"`). Add `SetReviewCycleCount(project, filename string, count int) error` to the `Store` interface.

2. **`config/planstore/sqlite.go`**:
   - Add migration constant: `const reviewCycleCountMigration = "ALTER TABLE plans ADD COLUMN review_cycle_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0"`
   - Call `migrateAddColumn(db, "review_cycle_count", reviewCycleCountMigration)` in `NewSQLiteStore`.
   - Add `review_cycle_count` to all SELECT column lists in queries (Get, List, ListByStatus, ListByTopic).
   - Add `review_cycle_count` to INSERT (Create) and UPDATE queries.
   - Update `scanPlanEntry` and `scanPlanEntries` to scan the new `int` column into `ReviewCycleCount`.
   - Implement `SetReviewCycleCount` method — targeted `UPDATE plans SET review_cycle_count = ? WHERE project = ? AND filename = ?` with not-found check (same pattern as `SetClickUpTaskID`).

3. **`config/planstore/http.go`**: Implement `SetReviewCycleCount` on `HTTPStore` — POST to the server endpoint with the count (same pattern as `SetClickUpTaskID`).

4. **`config/planstore/server.go`**: Add handler for `SetReviewCycleCount` requests — parse the count from the request body, call `store.SetReviewCycleCount()` (same pattern as ClickUp task ID handler).

5. **`config/planstate/planstate.go`**:
   - Add `ReviewCycleCount int` to `PlanEntry` (json tag: `"review_cycle_count,omitempty"`).
   - Map it in `Load()` from `planstore.PlanEntry.ReviewCycleCount`.
   - Map it in `toPlanstoreEntry()`.
   - Add `IncrementReviewCycleCount(filename string) (int, error)` method: reads current count, bumps by 1, updates local map, calls `store.SetReviewCycleCount()`, returns new count.
   - Add `GetReviewCycleCount(filename string) int` getter: returns `entry.ReviewCycleCount` or 0 if plan not found.

**Step 4: run test to verify it passes**

```bash
go test ./config/planstore/... -v
go test ./config/planstate/... -v
```

expected: PASS

**Step 5: commit**

```bash
git add config/planstore/ config/planstate/
git commit -m "feat: add ReviewCycleCount to planstore and planstate"
```

---

## Wave 2: Use Cycle Count in Instance Naming

> **depends on wave 1:** requires `ReviewCycleCount` field, `IncrementReviewCycleCount`, and `GetReviewCycleCount` methods from planstate

### Task 2: Wire cycle count into instance titles, opencode session titles, and serialization

This task modifies all the layers that construct instance names: the TUI spawn functions, the opencode session title builder, the Instance struct, and the serialization round-trip. These are tightly coupled — changing the title format in one place requires updating all others in the same commit to keep the build green.

**Files:**
- Modify: `app/app_state.go` (functions: `spawnReviewer`, `spawnCoderWithFeedback`, `spawnPlanAgent`)
- Modify: `internal/opencodesession/title.go`
- Modify: `session/instance_lifecycle.go` (function: `buildTitleOpts`)
- Modify: `session/instance.go` (add `ReviewCycleCount` field to `Instance`, `InstanceOptions`, `ToInstanceData`, `FromInstanceData`)
- Modify: `session/storage.go` (add `ReviewCycleCount` to `InstanceData`)
- Test: `internal/opencodesession/title_test.go`
- Test: `session/instance_title_test.go`

**Step 1: write the failing test**

Add test cases to `internal/opencodesession/title_test.go`:

```go
{
    name: "reviewer with cycle count",
    opts: TitleOpts{PlanName: "my-feature", AgentType: "reviewer", ReviewCycle: 2},
    want: "kas: review my-feature #2",
},
{
    name: "fixer with cycle count",
    opts: TitleOpts{PlanName: "my-feature", AgentType: "fixer", ReviewCycle: 1},
    want: "kas: fix my-feature #1",
},
```

Update `session/instance_title_test.go` reviewer test case to set `ReviewCycleCount: 1` on the instance and expect `"kas: review automatic-session-naming #1"`.

**Step 2: run test to verify it fails**

```bash
go test ./internal/opencodesession/... -run TestBuildTitle -v
go test ./session/... -run TestBuildTitleOptsFromInstance -v
```

expected: FAIL — `ReviewCycle` field doesn't exist on `TitleOpts`

**Step 3: write minimal implementation**

1. **`internal/opencodesession/title.go`**:
   - Add `ReviewCycle int` to `TitleOpts`.
   - In `BuildTitle`, after constructing the base title, if `opts.ReviewCycle > 0`, append ` #N` to the title (e.g. `"kas: review my-feature #2"`). This applies to all agent types but is only meaningful for reviewer/fixer.

2. **`session/instance.go`**:
   - Add `ReviewCycleCount int` field to `Instance` struct (comment: "ReviewCycleCount is the review cycle this instance belongs to (1-indexed, 0 = not a review cycle).").
   - Add `ReviewCycleCount int` to `InstanceOptions`.
   - Wire it through `NewInstance()`: `ReviewCycleCount: opts.ReviewCycleCount`.
   - Add it to `ToInstanceData()` and `FromInstanceData()`.

3. **`session/storage.go`**: Add `ReviewCycleCount int` to `InstanceData` struct (json tag: `"review_cycle_count,omitempty"`).

4. **`session/instance_lifecycle.go` — `buildTitleOpts()`**: Pass `inst.ReviewCycleCount` as `ReviewCycle` in the `TitleOpts`.

5. **`app/app_state.go` — `spawnReviewer()`**:
   - Before creating the instance, increment and persist: `cycle, _ := m.planState.IncrementReviewCycleCount(planFile)`.
   - Change title from `planName + "-review"` to `fmt.Sprintf("%s-review-%d", planName, cycle)`.
   - Pass `ReviewCycleCount: cycle` in `InstanceOptions` (not in the struct — set it on the instance after creation, or add to InstanceOptions).

6. **`app/app_state.go` — `spawnCoderWithFeedback()`**:
   - Read the current cycle count: `cycle := m.planState.GetReviewCycleCount(planFile)` (already incremented when the reviewer was spawned).
   - Change title from `planName + "-implement"` to `fmt.Sprintf("%s-fix-%d", planName, cycle)`.
   - Set `ReviewCycleCount: cycle` on the instance.

7. **`app/app_state.go` — `spawnPlanAgent()`**: For the `"review"` action (user-triggered from context menu), also use the cycle-counted title: increment, use `fmt.Sprintf("%s-review-%d", planName, cycle)`, set `ReviewCycleCount` on the instance. For `"implement"` action, keep the existing `planName + "-implement"` title (initial implementation is not a fix cycle).

**Step 4: run test to verify it passes**

```bash
go test ./internal/opencodesession/... -v
go test ./session/... -v
```

expected: PASS

**Step 5: commit**

```bash
git add app/app_state.go internal/opencodesession/ session/
git commit -m "feat: wire review cycle count into instance and session titles"
```

### Task 3: Update existing tests for new title format

All existing tests that assert on reviewer/fix-coder instance titles need updating to the new `-review-N`/`-fix-N` format. Tests that construct `home` structs with `planState` also need the plan entry to have a `ReviewCycleCount` that matches the expected title.

**Files:**
- Modify: `app/app_plan_completion_test.go`
- Modify: `app/app_wave_orchestration_flow_test.go`
- Modify: `app/clickup_progress_test.go`
- Modify: `app/app_reviewer_program_test.go`

**Step 1: write the failing test**

No new tests — this task fixes existing tests that broke from the title format change.

```bash
go test ./app/... -v -count=1 2>&1 | head -80
```

Identify all failures related to title assertions (e.g. `"feature-review"` vs `"feature-review-1"`).

Note: this task has no new failing test to write — it is a test-fix task. Steps 1-2 are replaced by running the existing suite and observing failures.

**Step 2: run test to verify it fails**

```bash
go test ./app/... -v -count=1
```

expected: FAIL — tests assert on old title format (`"feature-review"` instead of `"feature-review-1"`, `"feature-implement"` instead of `"feature-fix-1"`)

**Step 3: write minimal implementation**

1. **`app/app_plan_completion_test.go`**:
   - `TestReviewChangesSignal_RespawnsCoder`: update reviewer instance title from `"feature-review"` to `"feature-review-1"` and expect the spawned coder title to be `"feature-fix-1"`. Set `ReviewCycleCount: 1` on the plan entry in the test's planstate.
   - `TestReviewerTmuxDeath_DoesNotAutoApprove`: update reviewer title.
   - `TestCoderPushPrompt_*` tests: update coder title from `"test-feature-implement"` to `"test-feature-fix-1"` where the coder is spawned via feedback flow. Leave initial implementation titles as `"<name>-implement"`.
   - Any other tests that create reviewer instances with hardcoded titles.

2. **`app/app_wave_orchestration_flow_test.go`**: Update any reviewer/fix-coder title assertions if present.

3. **`app/clickup_progress_test.go`**: Update the `"fixer-hook-implement"` title and any reviewer titles to include cycle count.

4. **`app/app_reviewer_program_test.go`**: Update reviewer instance titles if this test creates reviewer instances.

The key pattern: wherever a test creates an instance with `Title: "X-review"` and `AgentType: AgentTypeReviewer`, change to `Title: "X-review-1"`. Wherever a test creates a feedback coder with `Title: "X-implement"`, change to `Title: "X-fix-1"`.

**Step 4: run test to verify it passes**

```bash
go test ./... -count=1
```

expected: PASS — all tests pass with updated title format

**Step 5: commit**

```bash
git add app/
git commit -m "fix: update test assertions for review cycle count title format"
```
